english: First Book of Adam and Eve

verses.t: On the third day, Aluahim planted the garden in the east of the earth, on the border of the world eastward, beyond which, towards the sun−rising, one finds nothing but water, that encompasses the whole world, and reaches to the borders of shamayim.
And to the north of the garden there is a sea of water, clear and pure to the taste, unlike anything Alse; so that, through the clearness thereof, one may look into the depths of the earth.
And when a man washes himself in it, he becomes clean of the cleanness thereof, and white of its whiteness −− even if he were dark.

And Aluahim created that sea of his own good pleasure, for He knew what would come of the man He would make; so that after he had left the garden, on account of his transgression, men should be born in the earth. Among them are righteous ones who will die, whose nepheshoth Aluahim would raise at the last day; when all of them will return to their flesh, bathe in the water of that sea, and repent of their sins. 5 But when Aluahim made Adawm go out of the garden, He did not place him on the border of it northward. This was so that he and Ḥawwah would not be able to go near to the sea of water where they could wash themselves in it, be cleansed from their sins, erase the transgression they had committed, and be no longer reminded of it in the thought of their punishment.

As to the southern side of the garden, Aluahim did not want Adawm to live there either; because, when the ruach blew from the north, it would bring him, on that southern side, the delicious smell of the trees of the garden.

Wherefore Aluahim did not put Adawm there. This was so that he would not be able to smell the sweet smell of those trees, forget his transgression, and find consolation for what he had done by taking delight in the smell of the trees First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter IThe crystal sea, Aluahim commands Adawm, expelled from Eden, to live in the Cave of Treasures.3 and yet not be cleansed from his transgression.

Again, also, because Aluahim is merciful and of great pity, and governs all things in a way that He alone knows −− He made our father Adawm live in the western border of the garden, because on that side the earth is very broad.
And Aluahim commanded him to live there in a cave in a rock −− the Cave of Treasures below the garden.
But when our father Adawm, and Ḥawwah, went out of the garden, they walked the ground on their feet, not knowing they were walking.

And when they came to the opening of the gate of the garden, and saw the broad earth spread before them, covered with stones large and small, and with sand, they feared and trembled, and fell on their faces, from the fear that came over them; and they were as dead.

Because −− whereas until this time they had been in the garden land, beautifully planted with all manner of trees −− they now saw themselves, in a strange land, which they knew not, and had never seen.
And because, when they were in the garden they were filled with the chen of a bright nature, and they had not hearts turned toward earthly things.
Therefore Aluahim had pity on them; and when He saw them fallen before the gate of the garden, He sent His Word to our father, Adawm and Ḥawwah, and raised them from their fallen state.

Aluahim said to Adawm, "I have ordained on this earth days and years, and you and your descendants shall live and walk in them, until the days and years are fulfilled; when I shall send the Word that created you, and against which you have transgressed, the Word that made you come out of the garden, and that raised you when you were fallen.
Yes, the Word that will again save you when the five and a half days are fulfilled."
But when Adawm heard these words from Aluahim, and of the great five and a half days, he did not understand the meaning of them.
For Adawm was thinking there would be only five and a half days for him until the end of the world.
And Adawm cried, and prayed to Aluahim to explain it to him. 6 Then Aluahim in his mercy for Adawm who was made after His own image and likeness, explained to him, that these were 5,000 and

But before that, Aluahim had made this berith with our father, Adawm, in the same terms, before he came out of the garden, when he was by the tree where Ḥawwah took of the fruit and gave it to him to eat. First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter IIAdawm and Ḥawwah faint when they leave 4
Because, when our father Adawm came out of the garden, he passed by that tree, and saw how Aluahim had changed the appearance of it into another form, and how it shriveled.
And as Adawm went to it he feared, trembled and fell down; but Aluahim in His mercy lifted him up, and then made this berith with him.

And again, when Adawm was by the gate of the garden, and saw the cherub with a sword of flashing fire in his hand, and the cherub grew angry and frowned at him, both Adawm and Ḥawwah became afraid of him, and thought he meant to put them to death. So they fell on their faces, trembled with fear.
But he had pity on them, and showed them mercy; and turning from them went up to shamayim, and prayed to Yahuah, and said; −−
"Yahuah, You sent me to watch at the gate of the garden, with a sword of fire.
But when Your servants, Adawm and Ḥawwah, saw me, they fell on their faces, and were as dead. O my Yahuah, what shall we do to Your servants?"
Then Aluahim had pity on them, and showed them mercy, and sent His Mal'ak to keep the garden.
And the Word of Yahuah came to Adawm and Ḥawwah, and raised them up.
And Yahuah said to Adawm, "I told you that at the end of the five and a half days, I will send my Word and save you.
Strengthen your heart, therefore, and stay in the Cave of Treasures, of which I have before spoken to you."
And when Adawm heard this Word from Aluahim, he was comforted with that which Aluahim had told him. For He had told him how He would save him.
But Adawm and Ḥawwah cried for having come out of the garden, their first home.
And indeed, when Adawm looked at his flesh, that was altered, he cried bitterly, he and Ḥawwah, over what they had done. And they walked and went gently down into the Cave of Treasures.
And as they came to it, Adawm cried over himself and said to Ḥawwah, "Look at this cave that is to be our prison in this world, and a place of punishment!
What is it compared with the garden? What is its narrowness compared with the space of the other?
What is this rock, by the side of those groves? What is the gloom of this cavern, compared with the light of the garden?

What is this overhanging ledge of rock to shelter us, compared with the mercy of Yahuah that overshadowed us? First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter IVAdawm mourns over the changed conditions. Adawm and Ḥawwah enter the Cave of Treasures. 5
What is the soil of this cave compared with the garden land? This earth, strewed with stones; and that, planted with delicious fruit trees?"
And Adawm said to Ḥawwah, "Look at your eyes, and at mine, which before beheld mal'akim praising in shamayim; and they too, without ceasing.
But now we do not see as we did; our eyes have become of flesh; they cannot see like they used to see before."
Adawm said again to Ḥawwah, "What is our body today, compared to what it was in former days, when we lived in the garden?"
After this, Adawm did not want to enter the cave, under the overhanging rock; nor would he ever want to enter it.
But he bowed to Aluahim's orders; and said to himself, "Unless I enter the cave, I shall again be a transgressor."
Then Adawm and Ḥawwah entered the cave, and stood praying, in their own tongue, unknown to us, but which they knew well.

And as they prayed, Adawm raised his eyes and saw the rock and the roof of the cave that covered him overhead. This prevented him from seeing either shamayim or Aluahim's creatures. So he cried and beat his chest hard, until he dropped, and was as dead.
And Ḥawwah sat crying; for she believed he was dead.
Then she got up, spread her hands toward Aluahim, appealing to Him for mercy and pity, and said, "O Aluahim, forgive me my sin, the sin which I committed, and don't remember it against me.
For I alone caused Your servant to fall from the garden into this condemned land; from light into this darkness; and from the house of joy into this prison.
O Aluahim, look at this Your servant fallen in this manner, and bring him back to life, that he may cry and repent of his transgression which he committed through me.
Don't take away his nephesh right now; but let him live that he may stand after the measure of his repentance, and do Your will, as before his death.

But if You do not bring him back to life, then, O Aluahim, take away my own nephesh, that I be like him, and leave me not in this dungeon, one and alone; for I could not stand alone in this world, but with him only.
For You, O Aluahim, caused him to fall asleep, and took a bone from his side, and restored the flesh in the place of it, by Your divine power.

And You took me, the bone, and make me a woman, bright like him, with heart, reason, and speech; and in flesh, like to his own; and You made me after the likeness of his looks, by Your mercy and power.

O Yahuah, I and he are one, and You, O Aluahim, are our Creator, You are He who made us both in one day. First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter V Ḥawwah makes a noble and emotional intercession, taking the blame on herself. 6
Therefore, O Aluahim, give him life, that he may be with me in this strange land, while we live in it on account of our transgression.
But if You will not give him life, then take me, even me, like him; that we both may die the same day."
And Ḥawwah cried bitterly, and fell on our father Adawm; from her great sorrow.
But Aluahim looked at them; for they had killed themselves through great grief. 2 But He decided to raise them and comfort them.
He, therefore, sent His Word to them; that they should stand and be raised immediately.
And Yahuah said to Adawm and Ḥawwah, "You transgressed of your own free will, until you came out of the garden in which I had placed you.

Of your own free will have you transgressed through your desire for divinity, greatness, and an exalted state, such as I have; so that I deprived you of the bright nature in which you then were, and I made you come out of the garden to this land, rough and full of trouble.

If only you had not transgressed My mitsvah and had kept My law, and had not eaten of the fruit of the tree which I told you not to come near! And there were fruit trees in the garden better than that one.

But the wicked Satan did not keep his faith and had no good intent towards Me, that although I had created him, he considered Me to be useless, and sought the Godhead for himself; for this I hurled him down from shamayim so that he could not remain in his first estate −− it was he who made the tree appear pleasant in your eyes, until you ate of it, by believing his words.
Thus have you transgressed My mitsvah, and therefore I have brought on you all these sorrows.
For I am Aluahim the Creator, who, when I created My creatures, did not intend to destroy them. But after they had sorely roused My anger, I punished them with grievous plagues, until they repent.
But, if on the contrary, they still continue hardened in their transgression, they shall be under a curse forever."

When Adawm and Ḥawwah heard these words from Aluahim, they cried and sobbed yet more; but they strengthened their hearts in Aluahim, because they now felt that Yahuah was to them like a father and a mother; and for this very reason, they cried before Him, and sought mercy from Him.

Then Aluahim had pity on them, and said: "O Adawm, I have made My berith with you, and I will not turn from it; neither will I let you return to the garden, until My berith of the great five and a half days is fulfilled."

Then Adawm said to Aluahim, "O Yahuah, You created us, and made us fit to be in the garden; and before I transgressed, You made all beasts come to me, that I should name them. First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter VIAluahim's reprimand to Adawm and Ḥawwah in which he points out how and why they sinned. 7
Your chen was then on me; and I named every one according to Your mind; and you made them all subject to me.
But now, O Yahuah Aluahim, that I have transgressed Your mitsvah, all beasts will rise against me and will devour me, and Ḥawwah Your handmaid; and will cut off our life from the face of the earth.
I therefore beg you, O Aluahim, that since You have made us come out of the garden, and have made us be in a strange land, You will not let the beasts hurt us."

When Yahuah heard these words from Adawm, He had pity on him, and felt that he had truly said that the beasts of the field would rise and devour him and Ḥawwah, because He, Yahuah, was angry with the two of them on account of their transgressions.

Then Aluahim commanded the beasts, and the birds, and all that moves on the earth, to come to Adawm and to be familiar with him, and not to trouble him and Ḥawwah; nor yet any of the good and righteous among their offspring.
Then all the beasts paid homage to Adawm, according to the mitsvah of Aluahim; except the serpent, against which Aluahim was angry. It did not come to Adawm, with the beasts.

Then Adawm cried and said, "O Aluahim, when we lived in the garden, and our hearts were lifted up, we saw the mal'akim that sang praises in shamayim, but now we can't see like we used to; no, when we entered the cave, all creation became hidden from us."

Then Aluahim Yahuah said to Adawm, "When you were under subjection to Me, you had a bright nature within you, and for that reason could you see things far away. But after your transgression your bright nature was withdrawn from you; and it was not left to you to see things far away, but only near at hand; after the ability of the flesh; for it is brutish."
When Adawm and Ḥawwah had heard these words from Aluahim, they went their way; praising and worshipping Him with a sorrowful heart.
And Aluahim ceased to commune with them.
Then Adawm and Ḥawwah came out of the Cave of Treasures, and went near to the garden gate, and there they stood to look at it, and cried for having come away from it.

And Adawm and Ḥawwah went from before the gate of the garden to the southern side of it, and found there the water that watered the garden, from the root of the Tree of Life, and that split itself from there into four rivers over the earth.
Then they came and went near to that water, and looked at it; and saw that it was the water that came forth from under the root of the Tree of Life in the garden.
And Adawm cried and wailed, and beat his chest, for being severed from the garden; and said to Ḥawwah: −− First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter VIIIThe "Bright Nature" of man is taken away. 8
"Why have you brought on me, on yourself, and on our descendants, so many of these plagues and punishments?"
And Ḥawwah said to him, "What is it you have seen that has caused you to cry and to speak to me in this manner?"
And he said to Ḥawwah, "Do you not see this water that was with us in the garden, that watered the trees of the garden, and flowed out from there?
And we, when we were in the garden, did not care about it; but since we came to this strange land, we love it, and turn it to use for our body."

But when Ḥawwah heard these words from him, she cried; and from the soreness of their crying, they fell into that water; and would have put an end to themselves in it, so as never again to return and behold the creation; for when they looked at the work of creation, they felt they must put an end to themselves.
Then Aluahim, merciful and gracious, looked at them thus lying in the water, and close to death, and sent an mal'ak, who brought them out of the water, and laid them on the seashore as dead.
Then the mal'ak went up to Aluahim, was welcome, and said, "O Aluahim, Your creatures have breathed their last."
Then Aluahim sent His Word to Adawm and Ḥawwah, who raised them from their death.
And Adawm said, after he was raised, "O Aluahim, while we were in the garden we did not require, or care for this water; but since we came to this land we cannot do without it."
Then Aluahim said to Adawm, "While you were under My command and were a bright mal'ak, you knew not this water.
But now that you have transgressed My mitsvah, you can not do without water, wherein to wash your body and make it grow; for it is now like that of beasts, and is in want of water."
When Adawm and Ḥawwah heard these words from Aluahim, they cried a bitter cry; and Adawm entreated Aluahim to let him return into the garden, and look at it a second time.
But Aluahim said to Adawm, "I have made you a promise; when that promise is fulfilled, I will bring you back into the garden, you and your righteous descendants."
And Aluahim ceased to commune with Adawm.
Then Adawm and Ḥawwah felt themselves burning with thirst, and heat, and sorrow.

And Adawm said to Ḥawwah, "We shall not drink of this water, even if we were to die. O Ḥawwah, when this water comes into our inner parts, it will increase our punishments and that of our descendants."

Both Adawm and Ḥawwah then went away from the water, and drank none of it at all; but came and entered the Cave of Treasures. First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter XTheir bodies need water after they leave the garden. 9
But when in it Adawm could not see Ḥawwah; he only heard the noise she made. Neither could she see Adawm, but heard the noise he made.
Then Adawm cried, in deep affliction, and beat his chest; and he got up and said to Ḥawwah, "Where are you?"
And she said to him, "Look, I am standing in this darkness."
He then said to her, "Remember the bright nature in which we lived, when we lived in the garden!
O Ḥawwah! Remember the glory that rested on us in the garden. O Ḥawwah! Remember the trees that overshadowed us in the garden while we moved among them.

O Ḥawwah! Remember that while we were in the garden, we knew neither night nor day. Think of the Tree of Life, from below which flowed the water, and that shed lustre over us! Remember, O Ḥawwah, the garden land, and the brightness thereof!
Think, oh think of that garden in which was no darkness, while we lived in it.
Whereas no sooner did we come into this Cave of Treasures than darkness surrounded us all around; until we can no longer see each other; and all the pleasure of this life has come to an end."
Then Adawm beat his chest, he and Ḥawwah, and they mourned the whole night until the crack of dawn, and they sighed over the length of the night in Miyazia.
And Adawm beat himself, and threw himself on the ground in the cave, from bitter grief, and because of the darkness, and lay there as dead.
But Ḥawwah heard the noise he made in falling on the ground. And she felt about for him with her hands, and found him like a corpse.
Then she was afraid, speechless, and remained by him.

But the merciful Yahuah looked on the death of Adawm, and on Ḥawwah's silence from fear of the darkness. 6 And the Word of Aluahim came to Adawm and raised him from his death, and opened Ḥawwah's mouth that she might speak.
Then Adawm stood up in the cave and said, "O Aluahim, why has light departed from us, and darkness covered us? Why did you leave us in this long darkness? Why do you plague us like this?
And this darkness, O Yahuah, where was it before it covered us? It is because of this that we cannot see each other.

For so long as we were in the garden, we neither saw nor even knew what darkness is. I was not hidden from Ḥawwah, neither was she hidden from me, until now that she cannot see me; and no darkness came over us to separate us from each other.

But she and I were both in one bright light. I saw her and she saw me. Yet now since we came into this cave, darkness has covered us, and separated us from each other, so that I do not see her, and she does not see me.
O Yahuah, will You then plague us with this darkness?" First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter XII How darkness came between Adawm and Ḥawwah. 10
Then when Aluahim, who is merciful and full of pity, heard Adawm's voice, He said to him: −−
"O Adawm, so long as the good mal'ak was obedient to Me, a bright light rested on him and on his hosts.
But when he transgressed My mitsvah, I deprived him of that bright nature, and he became dark.
And when he was in the shamayim, in the realms of light, he knew nothing of darkness.
But he transgressed, and I made him fall from the shamayim onto the earth; and it was this darkness that came over him.
And on you, O Adawm, while in My garden and obedient to Me, did that bright light rest also.

But when I heard of your transgression, I deprived you of that bright light. Yet, of My mercy, I did not turn you into darkness, but I made you your body of flesh, over which I spread this skin, in order that it may bear cold and heat.
If I had let My wrath fall heavily on you, I should have destroyed you; and had I turned you into darkness, it would have been as if I had killed you.

But in My mercy, I have made you as you are; when you transgressed My mitsvah, O Adawm, I drove you from the garden, and made you come forth into this land; and commanded you to live in this cave; and darkness covered you, as it did over him who transgressed My mitsvah.
Thus, O Adawm, has this night deceived you. It is not to last forever; but is only of twelve hours; when it is over, daylight will return.
Sigh not, therefore, neither be moved; and say not in your heart that this darkness is long and drags on wearily; and say not in your heart that I plague you with it.

Strengthen your heart, and be not afraid. This darkness is not a punishment. But, O Adawm, I have made the day, and have placed the sun in it to give light; in order that you and your children should do your work.

For I knew you would sin and transgress, and come out into this land. Yet I wouldn't force you, nor be heard over you, nor shut up; nor doom you through your fall; nor through your coming out from light into darkness; nor yet through your coming from the garden into this land.
For I made you of the light; and I willed to bring out children of light from you and like to you.
But you did not keep My mitsvah one day; until I had finished the creation and baruk everything in it.
Then, concerning the tree, I commanded you not to eat of it. Yet I knew that Satan, who deceived himself, would also deceive you.
So I made known to you by means of the tree, not to come near him. And I told you not to eat of the fruit thereof, nor to taste of it, nor yet to sit under it, nor to yield to it.

Had I not been and spoken to you, O Adawm, concerning the tree, and had I left you without a mitsvah, First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter XIIIThe fall of Adawm. Why night and day were created. 11 and you had sinned −− it would have been an offence on My part, for not having given you any order; you would turn around and blame Me for it.
But I commanded you, and warned you, and you fell. So that My creatures cannot blame Me; but the blame rests on them alone.

And, O Adawm, I have made the day so that you and your descendants can work and toil in it. And I have made the night for them to rest in it from their work; and for the beasts of the field to go forth by night and look for their food.
But little of darkness now remains, O Adawm, and daylight will soon appear."
Then Adawm said to Aluahim: "O Yahuah, take You my nephesh, and let me not see this gloom any more; or remove me to some place where there is no darkness."

But Aluahim Yahuah said to Adawm, "Indeed I say to you, this darkness will pass from you, every day I have determined for you, until the fulfillment of My berith; when I will save you and bring you back again into the garden, into the house of light you long for, in which there is no darkness*. I will bring you to it −− in the kingdom of shamayim."
Again said Aluahim to Adawm, "All this misery that you have been made to take on yourself because of your transgression, will not free you from the hand of Satan, and will not save you.

But I will. When I shall come down from shamayim, and shall become flesh of your descendants, and take on Myself the infirmity from which you suffer, then the darkness that covered you in this cave shall cover Me in the grave, when I am in the flesh of your descendants.
And I, who am without years, shall be subject to the reckoning of years, of times, of months, and of days, and I shall be reckoned as one of the sons of men, in order to save you."
And Aluahim ceased to commune with Adawm. * Reference: John 12:46

Then Adawm and Ḥawwah cried and sorrowed by reason of Aluahim's word to them, that they should not return to the garden until the fulfillment of the days decreed on them; but mostly because Aluahim had told them that He should suffer for their yasha.
After this, Adawm and Ḥawwah continued to stand in the cave, praying and crying, until the morning dawned on them.
And when they saw the light returned to them, they retrained from fear, and strengthened their hearts. First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter XIV The earliest prophesy of the coming of Mashiach. 12

Then Adawm began to come out of the cave. And when he came to the mouth of it, and stood and turned his face towards the east, and saw the sunrise in glowing rays, and felt the heat thereof on his body, he was afraid of it, and thought in his heart that this flame came forth to plague him.
He then cried and beat his chest, then he fell on the ground on his face and made his request, saying: −−
"O Yahuah, plague me not, neither consume me, nor yet take away my life from the earth."
For he thought the sun was Aluahim.

Because while he was in the garden and heard the voice of Aluahim and the sound He made in the garden, and feared Him, Adawm never saw the brilliant light of the sun, neither did its flaming heat touch his body.
Therefore he was afraid of the sun when flaming rays of it reached him. He thought Aluahim meant to plague him therewith all the days He had decreed for him.
For Adawm also said in his thoughts, as Aluahim did not plague us with darkness, behold, He has caused this sun to rise and to plague us with burning heat.
But while he was thinking like this in his heart, the Word of Aluahim came to him and said: −−

"O Adawm, get up on your feet. This sun is not Aluahim; but it has been created to give light by day, of which I spoke to you in the cave saying, 'that the dawn would come, and there would be light by day.'
But I am Aluahim who comforted you in the night."
And Aluahim ceased to commune with Adawm.
The Adawm and Ḥawwah came out at the mouth of the cave, and went towards the garden.

But as they went near it, before the western gate, from which Satan came when he deceived Adawm and Ḥawwah, they found the serpent that became Satan coming at the gate, and sorrowfully licking the dust, and wiggling on its breast on the ground, by reason of the curse that fell on it from Aluahim.
And whereas before the serpent was the most exalted of all beasts, now it was changed and become slippery, and the meanest of them all, and it crept on its breast and went on its belly.

And whereas it was the fairest of all beasts, it had been changed, and was become the ugliest of them all. Instead of feeding on the best food, now it turned to eat the dust. Instead of living, as before, in the best places, now it lived in the dust.
And, whereas it had been the most beautiful of all beasts, all of which stood dumb at its beauty, it was now abhorred of them.

And, again, whereas it lived in one beautiful home, to which all other animals came from Alsewhere; and where it drank, they drank also of the same; now, after it had become venomous, by reason of Aluahim's curse, all beasts fled from its home, and would not drink of the water it drank; but fled from it. First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter XVIIThe Chapter of the Serpent. 13
When the accursed serpent saw Adawm and Ḥawwah, it swelled its head, stood on its tail, and with eyes blood− red, acted like it would kill them.

It made straight for Ḥawwah, and ran after her; while Adawm standing by, cried because he had no stick in his hand with which to hit the serpent, and did not know how to put it to death. 3 But with a heart burning for Ḥawwah, Adawm approached the serpent, and held it by the tail; when it turned towards him and said to him: −−
"O Adawm, because of you and of Ḥawwah, I am slippery, and go on my belly." Then with its great strength, it threw down Adawm and Ḥawwah and squeezed them, and tried to kill them.
But Aluahim sent an mal'ak who threw the serpent away from them, and raised them up.
Then the Word of Aluahim came to the serpent, and said to it, "The first time I made you slick, and made you to go on your belly; but I did not deprive you of speech.
This time, however, you will be mute, and you and your race will speak no more; because, the first time My creatures were ruined because of you, and this time you tried to kill them."
Then the serpent was struck mute, and was no longer able to speak.
And a ruach blew down from shamayim by the command of Aluahim and carried away the serpent from Adawm and Ḥawwah, and threw it on the seashore where it landed in India.
But Adawm and Ḥawwah cried before Aluahim. And Adawm said to Him: −−
"O Yahuah, when I was in the cave, I said this to you, my Yahuah, the beasts of the field would rise and devour me, and cut off my life from the earth."
Then Adawm, because of what had happened to him, beat his chest and fell on the ground like a corpse. Then the Word of Aluahim came to him, who raised him, and said to him,

"O Adawm, not one of these beasts will be able to hurt you; because I have made the beasts and other moving things come to you in the cave. I did not let the serpent come with them because it might have risen against you and made you tremble; and the fear of it should fall into your hearts.
For I knew that the accursed one is wicked; therefore I would not let it come near you with the other beasts.
But now strengthen your heart and fear not. I am with you to the end of the days I have determined on you."

Then Adawm cried and said, "O Aluahim, take us away to some other place, where the serpent can not come near us again, and rise against us. For fear that it might find Your handmaid Ḥawwah alone and kill her; for its eyes are hideous and evil." First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter XVIIIThe mortal combat with the serpent. 14

But Aluahim said to Adawm and Ḥawwah, "From now on, don't be afraid, I will not let it come near you; I have driven it away from you, from this mountain; neither will I leave in it the ability to hurt you."
Then Adawm and Ḥawwah worshipped before Aluahim and gave Him thanks, and praised Him for having delivered them from death.
Then Adawm and Ḥawwah went in search of the garden.
And the heat beat like a flame on their faces; and they sweated from the heat, and cried before Yahuah.
But the place where they cried was close to a high mountain, facing the western gate of the garden.
Then Adawm threw himself down from the top of that mountain; his face was torn and his flesh was ripped; he lost a lot of blood and was close to death.
Meanwhile Ḥawwah remained standing on the mountain crying over him, thus lying.
And she said, "I don't wish to live after him; for all that he did to himself was through me."
Then she threw herself after him; and was torn and ripped by stones; and remained lying as dead.
But the merciful Aluahim, who looks over His creatures, looked at Adawm and Ḥawwah as they lay dead, and He sent His Word to them, and raised them.
And said to Adawm, "O Adawm, all this misery which you have brought on yourself, will have no affect against My rule, neither will it alter the berith of the 5,
Then Adawm said to Aluahim, "I dry up in the heat, I am faint from walking, and I don't want to be in this world. And I don't know when You will take me out of it to rest."
Then Yahuah Aluahim said to him, "O Adawm, it cannot be now, not until you have ended your days. Then shall I bring you out of this miserable land."

And Adawm said to Aluahim, "While I was in the garden I knew neither heat, nor languor, neither moving about, nor trembling, nor fear; but now since I came to this land, all this affliction has come over me.
Then Aluahim said to Adawm, "So long as you were keeping My mitsvah, My light and My chen rested on you. But when you transgressed My mitsvah, sorrow and misery came to you in this land."

And Adawm cried and said, "O Yahuah, do not cut me off for this, neither punish me with heavy plagues, nor yet repay me according to my sin; for we, of our own will, transgressed Your mitsvah, and ignored Your law, and tried to become Aluahims like you, when Satan the enemy deceived us." First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter XXIAdawm and Ḥawwah attempt suicide. 15

Then Aluahim said again to Adawm, "Because you have endured fear and trembling in this land, languor and suffering, treading and walking about, going on this mountain, and dying from it, I will take all this on Myself in order to save you."
Then Adawm cried more and said, "O Aluahim, have mercy on me, so far as to take on yourself, that which I will do."
But Aluahim withdrew His Word from Adawm and Ḥawwah.
Then Adawm and Ḥawwah stood on their feet; and Adawm said to Ḥawwah, "Strengthen yourself, and I also will strengthen myself." And she strengthened herself, as Adawm told her.

Then Adawm and Ḥawwah took stones and placed them in the shape of an mizbe'ach; and they took leaves from the trees outside the garden, with which they wiped, from the face of the rock, the blood they had spilled.
But that which had dropped on the sand, they took together with the dust with which it was mingled and offered it on the mizbe'ach as an offering to Aluahim.

Then Adawm and Ḥawwah stood under the Mizbe'ach and cried, thus praying to Aluahim, "Forgive us our trespass* and our sin, and look at us with Thine eye of mercy. For when we were in the garden our praises and our hymns went up before you without ceasing.

But when we came into this strange land, pure praise was not longer ours, nor righteous prayer, nor understanding hearts, nor sweet thoughts, nor just counsels, nor long discernment, nor upright feelings, neither is our bright nature left us. But our body is changed from the likeness in which it was at first, when we were created.
Yet now look at our blood which is offered on these stones, and accept it at our hands, like the praise we used to sing to you at first, when in the garden."
And Adawm began to make more requests of Aluahim. * ORIGINAL OF YAHUAH'S PRAYER SAID TO BE USED ABOUT

Then the merciful Aluahim, good and lover of men, looked at Adawm and Ḥawwah, and at their blood, which they had held up as an offering to Him; without an order from Him for so doing. But He wondered at them; and accepted their offerings.
And Aluahim sent from His presence a bright fire, that consumed their offering.
He smelled the sweet savor of their offering, and showed them mercy. First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter XXIIIAdawm and Ḥawwah strengthen themselves and make the first mizbe'ach ever built. 16

Then came the Word of Aluahim to Adawm, and said to him, "O Adawm, as you have shed your blood, so will I shed My own blood when I become flesh of your descendants; and as you died, O Adawm, so also will I die. And as you built an mizbe'ach, so also will I make for you an mizbe'ach of the earth; and as you offered your blood on it, so also will I offer My blood on an mizbe'ach on the earth.
And as you sued for forgiveness through that blood, so also will I make My blood forgiveness of sins, and erase transgressions in it.
And now, behold, I have accepted your offering, O Adawm, but the days of the berith in which I have bound you are not fulfilled. When they are fulfilled, then will I bring you back into the garden.
Now, therefore, strengthen your heart; and when sorrow comes over you, make Me an offering, and I will be favorable to you."
But Aluahim knew that Adawm believed he should frequently kill himself and make an offering to Him of his blood.
Therefore He said to him, "O Adawm, don't ever kill yourself like this again, by throwing yourself down from that mountain."

But Adawm said to Aluahim, "I was thinking to put an end to myself at once, for having transgressed Your mitsvot, and for my having come out of the beautiful garden; and for the bright light of which You have deprived me; and for the praises which poured forth from my mouth without ceasing, and for the light that covered me.
Yet of Your goodness, O Aluahim, do not get rid of me altogether; but be favorable to me every time I die, and bring me to life.

And thereby it will be made known that You are a merciful Aluahim, who does not want anyone to perish; who loves not that one should fall; and who does not condemn any one cruelly, badly, and by whole destruction."
Then Adawm remained silent.
And the Word of Aluahim came to him, and baruk him, and comforted him, and covenanted with him, that He would save him at the end of the days determined for him.
This, then, was the first offering Adawm made to Aluahim; and so it became his custom to do.

Then Adawm took Ḥawwah, and they began to return to the Cave of Treasures where they lived. But when they got closer to it and saw it from a distance, heavy sorrow fell on Adawm and Ḥawwah when they looked at it.

Then Adawm said to Ḥawwah, "When we were on the mountain we were comforted by the Word of Aluahim that conversed with us; and the light that came from the east shown over us. First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter XXVAluahim represented as merciful and loving. The establishing of worship. 17
But now the Word of Aluahim is hidden from us; and the light that shown over us is so changed as to disappear, and let darkness and sorrow come over us.
And we are forced to enter this cave which is like a prison, in which darkness covers us, so that we are separated from each other; and you can not see me, neither can I see you."
When Adawm had said these words, they cried and spread their hands before Aluahim; for they were full of sorrow.

And they prayed to Aluahim to bring the sun to them, to shine on them, so that darkness would not return to them, and that they wouldn't have to go under this covering of rock. And they wished to die rather than see the darkness.

Then Aluahim looked at Adawm and Ḥawwah and at their great sorrow, and at all they had done with a fervent heart, on account of all the trouble they were in, instead of their former well−being, and on account of all the misery that came over them in a strange land.
Therefore Aluahim was not angry with them; nor impatient with them; but he was patient and forbearing towards them, as towards the children He had created.

Then came the Word of Aluahim to Adawm, and said to him, "Adawm, as for the sun, if I were to take it and bring it to you, days, hours, years and months would all stop, and the berith I have made with you, would never be fulfilled.

But then you would be deserted and stuck in a perpetual plague, and you would never be saved. 11 Yes, rather, bear long and calm your nephesh while you live night and day; until the fulfillment of the days, and the time of My berith is come.
Then shall I come and save you, O Adawm, for I do not wish that you be afflicted.
And when I look at all the good things in which you lived, and why you came out of them, then would I willingly show you mercy.
But I cannot alter the berith that has gone out of My mouth; otherwise I would have brought you back into the garden.

When, however, the berith is fulfilled, then shall I show you and your descendants mercy, and bring you into a land of gladness, where there is neither sorrow nor suffering; but abiding joy and gladness, and light that never fails, and praises that never cease; and a beautiful garden that shall never pass away."
And Aluahim said again to Adawm, "Be patient and enter the cave, for the darkness, of which you were afraid, shall only be twelve hours long; and when ended, light shall come up."

Then when Adawm heard these words from Aluahim, he and Ḥawwah worshipped before Him, and their hearts were comforted. They returned into the cave after their custom, while tears flowed from their eyes, sorrow and wailing came from their hearts, and they wished their nephesh would leave their body.
And Adawm and Ḥawwah stood praying until the darkness of night came over them, and Adawm was hid from Ḥawwah, and she from him.
And they remained standing in prayer. First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter XXVAluahim represented as merciful and loving. The establishing of worship. 18
When Satan, the hater of all good, saw how they continued in prayer, and how Aluahim communed with them, and comforted them, and how He had accepted their offering −− Satan made an apparition.
He began with transforming his hosts; in his hands was a flashing fire, and they were in a great light.

He then placed his throne near the mouth of the cave because he could not enter into it by reason of their prayers. And he shed light into the cave, until the cave glistened over Adawm and Ḥawwah; while his hosts began to sing praises.

And Satan did this, in order that when Adawm saw the light, he should think within himself that it was a heavenly light, and that Satan's hosts were mal'akim; and that Aluahim had sent them to watch at the cave, and to give him light in the darkness.
So that when Adawm came out of the cave and saw them, and Adawm and Ḥawwah bowed to Satan, then he would overcome Adawm thereby, and a second time humble him before Aluahim.
When, therefore, Adawm and Ḥawwah saw the light, fancying it was real, they strengthened their hearts; yet, as they were trembling, Adawm said to Ḥawwah: −−

"Look at that great light, and at those many songs of praise, and at that host standing outside who won't come into our cave. Why don't they tell us what they want, where they are from, what the meaning of this light is, what those praises are, why they have been sent to this place, and why they won't come in?
If they were from Aluahim, they would come into the cave with us, and would tell us why they were sent."
Then Adawm stood up and prayed to Aluahim with a burning heart, and said: −−
"O Yahuah, is there in the world another Aluahim besides You, who created mal'akim and filled them with light, and sent them to keep us, who would come with them?

But, look, we see these hosts that stand at the mouth of the cave; they are in a great light; they sing loud praises. If they are of some other Aluahim than You, tell me; and if they are sent by you, inform me of the reason for which You have sent them."

No sooner had Adawm said this, than an mal'ak from Aluahim appeared to him in the cave, who said to him, "O Adawm, fear not. This is Satan and his hosts; he wishes to deceive you as he deceived you at first. For the first time, he was hidden in the serpent; but this time he is come to you in the likeness of an mal'ak of light; in order that, when you worshipped him, he might enslave you, in the very presence of Aluahim."

Then the mal'ak went from Adawm and seized Satan at the opening of the cave, and stripped him of the pretense he had assumed, and brought him in his own hideous form to Adawm and Ḥawwah; who were afraid of him when they saw him.

And the mal'ak said to Adawm, "This hideous form has been his ever since Aluahim made him fall from shamayim. He could not have come near you in it; he therefore transformed himself into an mal'ak of light." First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter XXVIIThe second tempting of Adawm and Ḥawwah. The devil takes on the form of a beguiling light.19
Then the mal'ak drove away Satan and his hosts from Adawm and Ḥawwah, and said to them, "Fear not; Aluahim who created you, will strengthen you."
And the mal'ak left them.
But Adawm and Ḥawwah remained standing in the cave; no consolation came to them; they divided in their thoughts.
And when it was morning they prayed; and then went out to seek the garden. For their hearts were towards it, and they could get no consolation for having left it.
But when the crafty Satan saw them, that they were going to the garden, he gathered together his host, and came in appearance on a cloud, intent on deceiving them.
But when Adawm and Ḥawwah saw him thus in a vision, they thought they were mal'akim of Aluahim come to comfort them about having left the garden, or to bring them back again into it.
And Adawm spread his hands before Aluahim, beseeching Him to make him understand what they were.
Then Satan, the hater of all good, said to Adawm, "O Adawm, I am an mal'ak of the great Aluahim; and, behold the hosts that surround me.

Aluahim has sent us to take you and bring you to the border of the garden northwards; to the shore of the clear sea, and bathe you and Ḥawwah in it, and raise you to your former gladness, that you return again to the garden."
These words sank into the heart of Adawm and Ḥawwah.

Yet Aluahim withheld His Word from Adawm, and did not make him understand at once, but waited to see his strength; whether he would be overcome as Ḥawwah was when in the garden, or whether he would prevail.
Then Satan called to Adawm and Ḥawwah, and said, "Behold, we go to the sea of water," and they began to go.
And Adawm and Ḥawwah followed them at some little distance.

But when they came to the mountain to the north of the garden, a very high mountain, without any steps to the top of it, the Devil drew near to Adawm and Ḥawwah, and made them go up to the top in reality, and not in a vision; wishing, as he did, to throw them down and kill them, and to wipe off their name from the earth; so that this earth should remain to him and his hosts alone.
But when the merciful Aluahim saw that Satan wished to kill Adawm with his many tricks, and saw that Adawm was meek and without guile, Aluahim spoke to Satan in a loud voice, and cursed him.

Then he and his hosts fled, and Adawm and Ḥawwah remained standing on the top of the mountain, from there they saw below them the wide world, high above which they were. But they saw none of the host which time after time were by them. First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter XXVIIIThe Devil pretends to lead Adawm and Ḥawwah to the water to bathe. 20
They cried, both Adawm and Ḥawwah, before Aluahim, and begged for forgiveness of Him.
Then came the Word from Aluahim to Adawm, and said to him, "Know you and understand concerning this Satan, that he seeks to deceive you and your descendants after you."
And Adawm cried before Yahuah Aluahim, and begged and prayed to Him to give him something from the garden, as a token to him, wherein to be comforted.
And Aluahim considered Adawm's thought, and sent the mal'ak Michael as far as the sea that reaches India, to take from there golden rods and bring them to Adawm.
This did Aluahim in His wisdom in order that these golden rods, being with Adawm in the cave, should shine forth with light in the night around him, and put an end to his fear of the darkness.
Then the mal'ak Michael went down by Aluahim's order, took golden rods, as Aluahim had commanded him, and brought them to Aluahim.

After these things, Aluahim commanded the mal'ak Gabriel to go down to the garden, and say to the cherub who kept it, "Behold, Aluahim has commanded me to come into the garden, and to take from it sweet smelling incense, and give it to Adawm."
Then the mal'ak Gabriel went down by Aluahim's order to the garden, and told the cherub as Aluahim had commanded him.
The cherub then said, "Well." And Gabriel went in and took the incense.
Then Aluahim commanded his mal'ak Raphael to go down to the garden, and speak to the cherub about some myrrh, to give to Adawm.
And the mal'ak Raphael went down and told the cherub as Aluahim had commanded him, and the cherub said, "Well." Then Raphael went in and took the myrrh.

The golden rods were from the Indian sea, where there are precious stones. The incense was from the eastern border of the garden; and the myrrh from the western border, from where bitterness came over Adawm.
And the mal'akim brought these things to Aluahim, by the Tree of Life, in the garden.

Then Aluahim said to the mal'akim, "Dip them in the spring of water; then take them and sprinkle their water over Adawm and Ḥawwah, that they be a little comforted in their sorrow, and give them to Adawm and Ḥawwah.

And the mal'akim did as Aluahim had commanded them, and they gave all those things to Adawm and Ḥawwah on the top of the mountain on which Satan had placed them, when he sought to make an end of them.

And when Adawm saw the golden rods, the incense and the myrrh, he was rejoiced and cried because he thought that the gold was a token of the kingdom from where he had come, that the incense was a token of the bright light which had been taken from him, and that the myrrh was a token of the sorrow in which he was. First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter XXXAdawm receives the first worldly goods. 21

After these things Aluahim said to Adawm, "You asked Me for something from the garden, to be comforted therewith, and I have given you these three tokens as a consolation to you; that you trust in Me and in My berith with you.

For I will come and save you; and kings shall bring me when in the flesh, gold, incense and myrrh; gold as a token of My kingdom; incense as a token of My divinity; and myrrh as a token of My suffering and of My death.
But, O Adawm, put these by you in the cave; the gold that it may shed light over you by night; the incense, that you smell its sweet savor; and the myrrh, to comfort you in your sorrow."
When Adawm heard these words from Aluahim, he worshipped before Him. He and Ḥawwah worshipped Him and gave Him thanks, because He had dealt mercifully with them.
Then Aluahim commanded the three mal'akim, Michael, Gabriel and Raphael, each to bring what he had brought, and give it to Adawm. And they did so, one by one.
And Aluahim commanded Suriyel and Salathiel to bear up Adawm and Ḥawwah, and bring them down from the top of the high mountain, and to take them to the Cave of Treasures.
There they laid the gold on the south side of the cave, the incense on the eastern side, and the myrrh on the western side. For the mouth of the cave was on the north side.
The mal'akim then comforted Adawm and Ḥawwah, and departed.
The gold was seventy rods*; the incense, twelve pounds; and the myrrh, three pounds.
These remained by Adawm in the Cave of Treasures**.
Aluahim gave these three things to Adawm on the third day after he had come out of the garden, in token of the three days Yahuah should remain in the heart of the earth.

And these three things, as they continued with Adawm in the cave, gave him light by night; and by day they gave him a little relief from his sorrow. * A rod is a unit of linear measure equivalent to 5.5 yards and also a unit of area measure equivalent to 30.25 square yards. In this case, the word rod simply means a kind of long, thin piece of gold of unspecified size and weight. ** This is the original text which appears to contain embedded editorial content: "These remained by Adawm in the House of Treasures; therefore was it called 'of concealment.' But other interpreters say it was called the 'Cave of Treasures,' by reason of the bodies of righteous men that were in it.

And Adawm and Ḥawwah remained in the Cave of Treasures until the seventh day; they neither ate of the fruit the earth, nor drank water. First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter XXXIThey make themselves more comfortable in the Cave of Treasures on the third day. 22
And when it dawned on the eighth day, Adawm said to Ḥawwah, "O Ḥawwah, we prayed Aluahim to give us something from the garden, and He sent his mal'akim who brought us what we had desired.

But now, get up, let us go to the sea of water we saw at first, and let us stand in it, praying that Aluahim will again be favorable to us and take us back to the garden; or give us something; or that He will give us comfort in some other land than this in which we are."
Then Adawm and Ḥawwah came out of the cave, went and stood on the border of the sea in which they had before thrown themselves, and Adawm said to Ḥawwah:−−
Come, go down into this place, and come not out of it until the end of thirty days, when I shall come to you. And pray to Aluahim with burning heart and a sweet voice, to forgive us.
And I will go to another place, and go down into it, and do like you."

Then Ḥawwah went down into the water, as Adawm had commanded her. Adawm also went down into the water; and they stood praying; and besought Yahuah to forgive them their offense, and to restore them to their former state.
And they stood like that praying, until the end of the thirty−five days.
But Satan, the hater of all good, sought them in the cave, but found them not, although he searched diligently for them.

But he found them standing in the water praying and thought within himself, "Adawm and Ḥawwah are standing like that in that water praying to Aluahim to forgive them their transgression, and to restore them to their former state, and to take them from under my hand.
But I will deceive them so that they shall come out of the water, and not fulfil their vow."
Then the hater of all good, went not to Adawm, but he went to Ḥawwah, and took the form of an mal'ak of Aluahim, praising and rejoicing, and said to her:−−

"Shalom be to you! Be glad and rejoice! Aluahim is favorable to you, and He sent me to Adawm. I have brought him the glad tidings of yasha, and of his being filled with bright light as he was at first.
And Adawm, in his joy for his restoration, has sent me to you, that you come to me, in order that I crown you with light like him.

And he said to me, 'Speak to Ḥawwah; if she does not come with you, tell her of the sign when we were on the top of the mountain; how Aluahim sent his mal'akim who took us and brought us to the Cave of Treasures; and laid the gold on the southern side; incense, on the eastern side; and myrrh on the western side.' Now come to him."
When Ḥawwah hear these words from him, she rejoiced greatly. And thinking Satan's appearance was real, she came out of the sea.

He went before, and she followed him until they came to Adawm. Then Satan hid himself from her, and she saw him no more. First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter XXXIII Satan falsely promises the "bright light." 23
She then came and stood before Adawm, who was standing by the water and rejoicing in Aluahim's forgiveness.
And as she called to him, he turned around, found her there and cried when he saw her, and beat his chest; and from the bitterness of his grief, he sank into the water.

But Aluahim looked at him and at his misery, and at his being about to breathe his last. And the Word of Aluahim came from shamayim, raised him out of the water, and said to him, "Go up the high bank to Ḥawwah." And when he came up to Ḥawwah he said to her, "Who told you to come here?"
Then she told him the discourse of the mal'ak who had appeared to her and had given her a sign.
But Adawm grieved, and gave her to know it was Satan. He then took her and they both returned to the cave.
These things happened to them the second time they went down to the water, seven days after their coming out of the garden.
They fasted in the water thirty−five days; altogether forty−two days since they had left the garden.

And on the morning of the forty−third day, they came out of the cave, sorrowful and crying. Their bodies were lean, and they were parched from hunger and thirst, from fasting and praying, and from their heavy sorrow on account of their transgression.
And when they had come out of the cave they went up the mountain to the west of the garden.
There they stood and prayed and besought Aluahim to grant them forgiveness of their sins.

And after their prayers Adawm began to beg Aluahim, saying, "O my Yahuah, my Aluahim, and my Creator, You commanded the four Alements* to be gathered together, and they were gathered together by Thine order.
Then You spread Your hand and created me out of one Alement, that of dust of the earth; and You brought me into the garden at the third hour, on a Friday, and informed me of it in the cave.
Then, at first, I knew neither night nor day, for I had a bright nature; neither did the light in which I lived ever leave me to know night or day.

Then, again, O Yahuah, in that third hour in which You created me, You brought to me all beasts, and lions, and ostriches, and fowls of the air, and all things that move in the earth, which You had created at the first hour before me of the Friday.

And Your will was that I should name them all, one by one, with a suitable name. But You gave me understanding and knowledge, and a pure heart and a right mind from you, that I should name them after Thine own mind regarding the naming of them.

O Aluahim, You made them obedient to me, and ordered that not one of them break from my sway, according to Your mitsvah, and to the dominion which You had given me over them. But now they are all estranged from me. First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter XXXIVAdawm recalls the creation of Ḥawwah. He Aloquently appeals for food and drink. 24

Then it was in that third hour of Friday, in which You created me, and commanded me concerning the tree, to which I was neither to go near, nor to eat thereof; for You said to me in the garden, 'When you eat of it, of death you shall die.'
And if You had punished me as You said, with death, I should have died that very moment.

Moreover, when You commanded me regarding the tree, I was neither to approach nor to eat thereof, Ḥawwah was not with me; You had not yet created her, neither had You yet taken her out of my side; nor had she yet heard this order from you.
Then, at the end of the third hour of that Friday, O Yahuah, You caused a slumber and a sleep to come over me, and I slept, and was overwhelmed in sleep.

Then You drew a rib out of my side, and created it after my own likeness and image. Then I awoke; and when I saw her and knew who she was, I said, 'This is bone of my bones, and flesh of my flesh; from now on she shall be called woman.'

It was of Your good will, O Aluahim, that You brought a slumber in a sleep over me, and that You immediately brought Ḥawwah out of my side, until she was out, so that I did not see how she was made; neither could I witness, O my Yahuah, how awful and great are Your goodness and glory.

And of Your goodwill, O Yahuah, You made us both with bodies of a bright nature, and You made us two, one; and You gave us Your chen, and filled us with praises of the Ruach haQodesh; that we should be neither hungry nor thirsty, nor know what sorrow is, nor yet faintness of heart; neither suffering, fasting nor weariness.
But now, O Aluahim, since we transgressed Your mitsvah and broke Your law, You have brought us out into a strange land, and have caused suffering, and faintness, hunger and thirst to come over us.
Now, therefore, O Aluahim, we pray you, give us something to eat from the garden, to satisfy our hunger with it; and something wherewith to quench our thirst.
For, behold, many days, O Aluahim, we have tasted nothing and drunk nothing, and our flesh is dried up, and our strength is wasted, and sleep is gone from our eyes from faintness and crying.
Then, O Aluahim, we dare not gather anything from the fruit of trees, from fear of you. For when we transgress at first You spared us and did not make us die.
But now, we thought in our hearts, if we eat of the fruit of the trees, without Aluahim's order, He will destroy us this time, and will wipe us off from the face of the earth.
And if we drink of this water, without Aluahim's order, He will make an end of us and root us up at once.
Now, therefore, O Aluahim, that I am come to this place with Ḥawwah, we beg You to give us some fruit from the garden, that we may be satisfied with it.

Then Aluahim looked again at Adawm and his crying and groaning, and the Word of Aluahim came to him, and said to him: −−

"O Adawm, when you were in My garden, you knew neither eating nor drinking; neither faintness nor suffering; neither leanness of flesh, nor change; neither did sleep depart from thine eyes. But since you transgressed, and came into this strange land, all these trials are come over you."
Then Aluahim commanded the cherub, who kept the gate of the garden with a sword of fire in his hand, to take some of the fruit of the fig−tree, and to give it to Adawm.

The cherub obeyed the command of Yahuah Aluahim, and went into the garden and brought two figs on two twigs, each fig hanging to its leaf; they were from two of the trees among which Adawm and Ḥawwah hid themselves when Aluahim went to walk in the garden, and the Word of Aluahim came to Adawm and Ḥawwah and said to them, "Adawm, Adawm, where are you?"
And Adawm answered, "O Aluahim, here I am. When I heard the sound of You and Your voice, I hid myself, because I am naked."

Then the cherub took two figs and brought them to Adawm and Ḥawwah. But he threw them to them from a distance; for they might not come near the cherub by reason of their flesh, that could not come near the fire.
At first, mal'akim trembled at the presence of Adawm and were afraid of him. But now Adawm trembled before the mal'akim and was afraid of them.
Then Adawm came closer and took one fig, and Ḥawwah also came in turn and took the other.
And as they took them up in their hands, they looked at them, and knew they were from the trees among which they had hidden themselves.

Then Adawm said to Ḥawwah, "Do you not see these figs and their leaves, with which we covered ourselves when we were stripped of our bright nature? But now, we do not know what misery and suffering may come over us from eating them.
Now, therefore, O Ḥawwah, let us restrain ourselves and not eat of them, you and I; and let us ask Aluahim to give us of the fruit of the Tree of Life."
Thus did Adawm and Ḥawwah restrain themselves, and did not eat of these figs.

But Adawm began to pray to Aluahim and to beseech Him to give him of the fruit of the Tree of Life, saying thus: "O Aluahim, when we transgressed Your mitsvah at the sixth hour of Friday, we were stripped of the bright nature we had, and did not continue in the garden after our transgression, more than three hours. But in the evening You made us come out of it. O Aluahim, we transgressed against You one hour, and all these trials and sorrows have come over us until this day.
And those days together with this the forty−third day, do not redeem that one hour in which we transgressed!
O Aluahim, look at us with an eye of pity, and do not avenge us according to our transgression of Your mitsvah, in Your presence.
O Aluahim, give us of the fruit of the Tree of Life, that we may eat of it, and live, and turn not to see sufferings and other trouble, in this earth; for You are Aluahim.

When we transgressed Your mitsvah, You made us come out of the garden, and sent a cherub to keep the Tree of Life, lest we should eat thereof, and live; and know nothing of faintness after we transgressed.
But now, O Yahuah, behold, we have endured all these days, and have borne sufferings. Make these forty−three days an equivalent for the one hour in which we transgressed."
After these things the Word of Aluahim came to Adawm, and said to him: −−

But these forty−three days cannot make amends for the hour in which you transgressed My mitsvah.

And Aluahim withdrew His Word from Adawm.

But Adawm said to Ḥawwah, "I am afraid to eat of this fig. I know not what may come over me from it."

So Adawm cried, and stood praying before Aluahim, saying, "Satisfy my hunger, without my having to eat this fig; for after I have eaten it, what will it profit me? And what shall I desire and ask of you, O Aluahim, when it is gone?"

Then Adawm took the fig, and laid it on the golden rods. Ḥawwah also took her fig, and put it on the incense. 2 And the weight of each fig was that of a water−melon; for the fruit of the garden was much larger than the fruit of this land*.
But Adawm and Ḥawwah remained standing and fasting the whole of that night, until the morning dawned.

For Aluahim has not fed us with the Tree of Life, in order that we may not live. Therefore, we will ask him to give us some of the Water of Life, and to quench our thirst with it, rather than with a drink of water of this land."
When Ḥawwah heard these words from Adawm, she agreed; and they both got up and came to the southern border of the garden, at the edge of the river of water a short distance from the garden.
And they stood and prayed before Yahuah, and asked Him to look at them this once, to forgive them, and to grant them their request.
After this prayer from both of them, Adawm began to pray with his voice before Aluahim, and said; −−

"O Yahuah, when I was in the garden and saw the water that flowed from under the Tree of Life, my heart did not desire, neither did my body require to drink of it; neither did I know thirst, for I was living; and above that which I am now.
So that in order to live I did not require any Food of Life, neither did I drink of the Water of Life.
But now, O Aluahim, I am dead; my flesh is parched with thirst. Give me of the Water of Life that I may drink of it and live.

Of Your mercy, O Aluahim, save me from these plagues and trials, and bring me into another land different from this, if You will not let me live in Your garden." * This is substantiated by Genesis 3:7 whereby the leaves of the fig tree were large enough that Adawm and Ḥawwah could fashion garments from them. Then Adawm said no more. And they kept looking, He and Ḥawwah, towards the cave, and at the fire that flared up around it.
But that fire was from Satan. For he had gathered trees and dry grasses, and had carried and brought them to the cave, and had set fire to them, in order to consume the cave and what was in it.
So that Adawm and Ḥawwah should be left in sorrow, and he should cut off their trust in Aluahim, and make them deny Him.
But by the mercy of Aluahim he could not burn the cave, for Aluahim sent His mal'ak around the cave to guard it from such a fire, until it went out.
And this fire lasted from noon−day until the break of day. That was the forty−fifth day.
Yet Adawm and Ḥawwah were standing and looking at the fire, and unable to come near the cave from their dread of the fire.

And Satan kept on bringing trees and throwing them into the fire, until the flames of the fire rose up on high, First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter XLIVThe power of fire over man. 30 and covered the whole cave, thinking, as he did in his own mind, to consume the cave with much fire. But the mal'ak of Yahuah was guarding it.
And yet he could not curse Satan, nor injure him by word, because he had no authority over him, neither did he take to doing so with words from his mouth.

Therefore the mal'ak tolerated him, without saying one bad word, until the Word of Aluahim came who said to Satan, "Go away from here; once before you deceived My servants, and this time you seek to destroy them.
Were it not for My mercy I would have destroyed you and your hosts from off the earth. But I have had patience with you, until the end of the world."

Then Satan fled from before Yahuah. But the fire went on burning around the cave like a coal−fire the whole day; which was the forty−sixth day Adawm and Ḥawwah had spent since they came out of the garden.

And when Adawm and Ḥawwah saw that the heat of the fire had somewhat cooled down, they began to walk towards the cave to get into it as they usually did; but they could not, by reason of the heat of the fire.
Then they both began crying because of the fire that separated them from the cave, and that came towards them, burning. And they were afraid.

Then Adawm said to Ḥawwah, "See this fire of which we have a portion in us: which formerly yielded to us, but no longer does so, now that we have transgressed the limit of creation, and changed our condition, and our nature is altered. But the fire is not changed in its nature, nor altered from its creation. Therefore it now has power over us; and when we come near it, it scorches our flesh."
Then Adawm rose and prayed to Aluahim, saying, "See, this fire has separated us from the cave in which You have commanded us to live; but now, behold, we cannot go into it."
Then Aluahim heard Adawm, and sent him His Word, that said: −−
"O Adawm, see this fire! How different the flame and heat thereof are from the garden of delights and the good things in it!

Aluahim said again to him, "See, O Adawm, how Satan has exalted you! He has deprived you of the Godhead, and of an exalted state like Me, and has not kept his word to you; but has, after all, become your enemy. He is the one who made this fire in which he meant to burn you and Ḥawwah.
Why, O Adawm, has he not kept his agreement with you, not even one day; but has deprived you of the glory that was on you −− when you yielded to his command?
Do you think, Adawm, that he loved you when he made this agreement with you? Or that he loved you and wished to raise you on high?

Then you will see the burning of his fire, that will be burning around you and likewise your descendants. "O, our Yahuah, what will you do?"

But get up and pray to Aluahim to let us know concerning this, and what this rock is that is spread over us like a tent."

Then Adawm stood up and prayed before Yahuah, to let him know what had brought about this difficult time. And Adawm stood praying like that until the morning. * The word "dome" is used here but the text does not specifically suggest that the covering was roundonly that it covered them on all sides, however a dome is the most likely shape that would have be able to withstand the impact with the ground. From verse
When Adawm heard these words from Aluahim, he took Ḥawwah and went from the northern end of the garden to the south of it, by the river of water where they once fasted.
But as they were going on their way, and before they got there, Satan, the wicked one, had heard the Word of Aluahim communing with Adawm respecting his covering.

It grieved him, and he hastened to the place where the sheep−skins were, with the intention of taking them and throwing them into the sea, or of burning them with fire, so that Adawm and Ḥawwah would not find them.

But as he was about to take them, the Word of Aluahim came from shamayim, and bound him by the side of those skins until Adawm and Ḥawwah came near him. But as they got closer to him they were afraid of him, and of his hideous look.

Then came the Word of Aluahim to Adawm and Ḥawwah, and said to them, "This is he who was hidden in the serpent, and who deceived you, and stripped you of the garment of light and glory in which you were.
This is he who promised you majesty and divinity. Where, then, is the beauty that was on him? Where is his divinity? Where is his light? Where is the glory that rested on him?
Now his figure is hideous; he is become abominable among mal'akim; and he has come to be called Satan.
O Adawm, he wished to take from you this earthly garment of sheep−skins, and to destroy it, and not let you be covered with it.
What, then, is his beauty that you should have followed him? And what have you gained by obeying him? See his evil works and then look at Me; at Me, your Creator, and at the good deeds I do you.
See, I bound him until you came and saw him and beheld his weakness, that no power is left with him."
And Aluahim released him from his bonds.
After this Adawm and Ḥawwah said no more, but cried before Aluahim on account of their creation, and of their bodies that required an earthly covering.

Then Adawm said to Ḥawwah, "O Ḥawwah, this is the skin of beasts with which we shall be covered, but when we put it on, behold, we shall be wearing a token of death on our bodies. Just as the owners of these skins have died and First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter XLVIverse 36 have wasted away, so also shall we die and pass away."
Then Adawm and Ḥawwah took the skins, and went back to the Cave of Treasures; and when in it, they stood and prayed as they were accustomed.
And they thought how they could make garments of those skins; for they had no skill for it.

Then Aluahim sent to them His mal'ak to show them how to work it out. And the mal'ak said to Adawm, "Go forth, and bring some palm−thorns." Then Adawm went out, and brought some, as the mal'ak had commanded him.
Then the mal'ak began before them to work out the skins, after the manner of one who prepares a shirt. And he took the thorns and stuck them into the skins, before their eyes.
Then the mal'ak again stood up and prayed Aluahim that the thorns in those skins should be hidden, so as to be, as it were, sewn with one thread.
And so it was, by Aluahim's order; they became garments for Adawm and Ḥawwah, and He clothed them therewith.
From that time the nakedness of their bodies was covered from the sight of each other's eyes.
And this happened at the end of the fifty−first day.

Then when Adawm's and Ḥawwah's bodies were covered, they stood and prayed, and sought mercy of Yahuah, and forgiveness, and gave Him thanks for that He had had mercy on them, and had covered their nakedness. And they ceased not from prayer the whole of that night.
Then when the morning dawned at the rising of the sun, they said their prayers after their custom; and then went out of the cave.
And Adawm said to Ḥawwah, "Since we don't know what there is to the west of this cave, let us go out and see it today." Then they came forth and went toward the western border.

They were not very far from the cave, when Satan came towards them, and hid himself between them and the cave, under the form of two ravenous lions three days without food, that came towards Adawm and Ḥawwah, as if to break them in pieces and devour them.
Then Adawm and Ḥawwah cried, and prayed Aluahim to deliver them from their paws.
Then the Word of Aluahim came to them, and drove away the lions from them.
And Aluahim said to Adawm, "O Adawm, what do you seek on the western border? And why have you left of thine own accord the eastern border, in which was your living place?
Now then, turn back to your cave, and remain in it, so that Satan won't deceive you or work his purpose over you.

For in this western border, O Adawm, there will go from you a descendant, that shall replenish it; and that will defile themselves with their sins, and with their yielding to the commands of Satan, and by following his works. 7 First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter LIIIThe neḇuah of the Western Lands and of the great flood. 37 Therefore will I bring over them the waters of a flood, and overwhelm them all. But I will deliver what is left of the righteous among them; and I will bring them to a distant land, and the land in which you live now shall remain desolate and without one inhabitant in it.

After Aluahim had thus spoken to them, they went back to the Cave of Treasures. But their flesh was dried up, and they were weak from fasting and praying, and from the sorrow they felt at having trespassed against Aluahim.

Then Adawm and Ḥawwah stood up in the cave and prayed the whole of that night until the morning dawned. And when the sun was risen they both went out of the cave; their heads were wandering from heaviness of sorrow and they didn't know where they were going.

And they walked in that condition to the southern border of the garden. And they began to go up that border until they came to the eastern border beyond which there was no more land. 3 And the cherub who guarded the garden was standing at the western gate, and guarding it against Adawm and Ḥawwah, lest they should suddenly come into the garden. And the cherub turned around, as if to put them to death; according to the mitsvah Aluahim had given him. 4 When Adawm and Ḥawwah came to the eastern border of the garden −− thinking in their hearts that the cherub was not watching −− as they were standing by the gate as if wishing to go in, suddenly came the cherub with a flashing sword of fire in his hand; and when he saw them, he went forth to kill them. For he was afraid that Aluahim would destroy him if they went into the garden without His order.
And the sword of the cherub seemed to shoot flames a distance away from it. But when he raised it over Adawm and Ḥawwah, the flame of the sword did not flash forth.
Therefore the cherub thought that Aluahim was favorable to them, and was bringing them back into the garden. And the cherub stood wondering.

He could not go up to Shamayim to determine Aluahim's order regarding their getting into the garden; he therefore continued to stand by them, unable as he was to part from them; for he was afraid that if they should enter the garden without permission, Aluahim would destroy him.
When Adawm and Ḥawwah saw the cherub coming towards them with a flaming sword of fire in his hand, they fell on their faces from fear, and were as dead.
At that time the shamayim and the earth shook; and another cherubim came down from shamayim to the cherub who guarded the garden, and saw him amazed and silent.
Then, again, other mal'akim came down close to the place where Adawm and Ḥawwah were. They were divided between joy and sorrow.
They were glad, because they thought that Aluahim was favorable to Adawm, and wished him to return to the garden; and wished to restore him to the gladness he once enjoyed.

But they sorrowed over Adawm, because he was fallen like a dead man, he and Ḥawwah; and they said in their thoughts, "Adawm has not died in this place; but Aluahim has put him to death, for his having come to this place, and wishing to get into the garden without His permission." First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter LIVAdawm and Ḥawwah go exploring. 38

Then came the Word of Aluahim to Adawm and Ḥawwah, and raised them from their dead state, saying to them, "Why did you come up here? Do you intend to go into the garden, from which I brought you out? It cannot be today; but only when the berith I have made with you is fulfilled."

Then Adawm, when he heard the Word of Aluahim, and the fluttering of the mal'akim whom he did not see, but only heard the sound of them with his ears, he and Ḥawwah cried, and said to the mal'akim: −−

"O Ruchot, who wait on Aluahim, look at me, and at my being unable to see you! For when I was in my former bright nature, then I could see you. I sang praises as you do; and my heart was far above you.

But now, that I have transgressed, that bright nature is gone from me, and I am come to this miserable state. And now I have come to this, that I cannot see you, and you do not serve me like you used to do. For I have become animal flesh.

Yet now, O mal'akim of Aluahim, ask Aluahim with me, to restore me to that wherein I was formerly; to rescue me from this misery, and to remove from me the sentence of death He passed on me, for having trespassed against Him."

Then, when the mal'akim heard these words, they all grieved over him; and cursed Satan who had misled Adawm, until he came from the garden to misery; from life to death; from shalom to trouble; and from gladness to a strange land.
Then the mal'akim said to Adawm, "You obeyed Satan, and ignored the Word of Aluahim who created you; and you believed that Satan would fulfil all he had promised you.
But now, O Adawm, we will make known to you, what came over us though him, before his fall from shamayim.
