He gathered together his hosts, and deceived them, promising to give them a great kingdom, a divine nature; and other promises he made them. 10 His hosts believed that his word was true, so they yielded to him, and renounced the glory of Aluahim.
He then sent for us −− according to the orders in which we were −− to come under his command, and to accept his vein promise. But we would not, and we did not take his advice.

Then after he had fought with Aluahim, and had dealt forwardly with Him, he gathered together his hosts, and made war with us. And if it had not been for Aluahim's strength that was with us, we could not have prevailed against him to hurl him from shamayim.
But when he fell from among us, there was great joy in shamayim, because of his going down from us. For if he had remained in shamayim, nothing, not even one mal'ak would have remained in it.
But Aluahim in His mercy, drove him from among us to this dark earth; for he had become darkness itself and a worker of unrighteousness.

And he has continued, O Adawm, to make war against you, until he tricked you and made you come out of the garden, to this strange land, where all these trials have come to you. And death, which Aluahim brought to him, he has also brought to you, O Adawm, because you obeyed him, and trespassed against Aluahim."

Then all the mal'akim rejoiced and praised Aluahim, and asked Him not to destroy Adawm this time, for his having First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter LVThe Conflict between Aluahim and Satan. 39 sought to enter the garden; but to bear with him until the fulfillment of the promise; and to help him in this world until he was free from Satan's hand.
Then came the Word of Aluahim to Adawm, and said to him: −−
"O Adawm, look at that garden of joy and at this earth of toil, and behold the garden is full of mal'akim, but look at yourself alone on this earth with Satan whom you obeyed.
Yet, if you had submitted, and been obedient to Me, and had kept My Word, you would be with My mal'akim in My garden.
But when you transgressed and obeyed Satan, you became his guests among his mal'akim, that are full of wickedness; and you came to this earth, that brings forth to you thorns and thistles.

O Adawm, ask him who deceived you, to give you the divine nature he promised you, or to make you a garden as I had made for you; or to fill you with that same bright nature with which I had filled you.

Ask him to make you a body like the one I made you, or to give you a day of rest as I gave you; or to create within you a reasonable nephesh, as I created for you; or to take you from here to some other earth than this one which I gave you. But, O Adawm, he will not fulfil even one of the things he told you.

Acknowledge, then, My chen towards you, and My mercy on you, My creature; that I have not avenged you for your transgression against Me, but in My pity for you I have promised you that at the end of the great five and a half days I will come and save you."
Then Aluahim said again to Adawm and Ḥawwah, "Get up, go down from here, before the cherub with a sword of fire in his hand destroys you."
But Adawm's heart was comforted by Aluahim's words to him, and he worshipped before Him.
And Aluahim commanded His mal'akim to escort Adawm and Ḥawwah to the cave with joy, instead of the fear that had come over them.

Then the mal'akim took up Adawm and Ḥawwah, and brought them down from the mountain by the garden, with songs and psalms, until they arrived at the cave. There the mal'akim began to comfort and to strengthen them, and then departed from them towards shamayim, to their Creator, who had sent them.

But after the mal'akim had departed from Adawm and Ḥawwah, Satan came with shamefacedness, and stood at the entrance of the cave in which were Adawm and Ḥawwah. He then called to Adawm, and said, "O Adawm, come, let me speak to you."
Then Adawm came out of the cave, thinking he was one of Aluahim's mal'akim that was come to give him some good counsel.
But when Adawm came out and saw his hideous figure, he was afraid of him, and said to him, "Who are you?" First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter LVIA Chapter of divine comfort. 40

Then Satan answered and said to him, "It is I, who hid myself within the serpent, and who spoke to Ḥawwah, and who enticed her until she obeyed my command. I am he who sent her, using my deceitful speech, to deceive you, until you both ate of the fruit of the tree and abandoned the command of Aluahim."
But when Adawm heard these words from him, he said to him, "Can you make me a garden as Aluahim made for me? Or can you clothe me in the same bright nature in which Aluahim had clothed me?
Where is the divine nature you promised to give me? Where is that slick speech of yours that you had with us at first, when we were in the garden?"

Then Satan said to Adawm, "Do you think that when I have promised one something that I would actually deliver it to him or fulfil my word? Of course not. For I myself have never even thought of obtaining what I promised.
Therefore I fell, and I made you fall by that for which I myself fell; and with you also, whosoever accepts my counsel, falls thereby.

But now, O Adawm, because you fell you are under my rule, and I am king over you; because you have obeyed me and have transgressed against your Aluahim. Neither will there be any deliverance from my hands until the day promised you by your Aluahim."

Again he said, "Because we do not know the day agreed on with you by your Aluahim, nor the hour in which you shall be delivered, for that reason we will multiply war and murder on you and your descendants after you.
This is our will and our good pleasure, that we may not leave one of the sons of men to inherit our orders in shamayim.

For as to our home, O Adawm, it is in burning fire; and we will not stop our evil doing, no, not one day nor one hour. And I, O Adawm, shall set you on fire when you come into the cave to live there."
When Adawm heard these words he cried and mourned, and said to Ḥawwah, "Hear what he said; that he won't fulfil any of what he told you in the garden. Did he really then become king over us?
But we will ask Aluahim, who created us, to deliver us out of his hands."
Then Adawm and Ḥawwah spread their hands before Aluahim, praying and begging Him to drive Satan away from them so that he can't harm them or force them to deny Aluahim.
Then Aluahim sent to them at once, His mal'ak, who drove away Satan from them. This happened about sunset, on the fifty−third day after they had come out of the garden.
Then Adawm and Ḥawwah went into the cave, and stood up and turned their faces to the ground, to pray to Aluahim.

But before they prayed Adawm said to Ḥawwah, "Look, you have seen what temptations have befallen us in this land. Come, let us get up, and ask Aluahim to forgive us the sins we have committed; and we will not come out until the end of the day next to the fortieth. And if we die in here, He will save us."
Then Adawm and Ḥawwah got up, and joined together in entreating Aluahim. First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter LVIII"About sunset on the 53rd day. . ." 41
They continued praying like this in the cave; neither did they come out of it, by night or by day, until their prayers went up out of their mouths, like a flame of fire.

But Satan, the hater of all good, did not allow them to finish their prayers. For he called to his hosts, and they came, all of them. Then he said to them, "Since Adawm and Ḥawwah, whom we deceived, have agreed together to pray to Aluahim night and day, and to beg Him to deliver them, and since they will not come out of the cave until the end of the fortieth day.

And since they will continue their prayers as they have both agreed to do, that He will deliver them out of our hands, and restore them to their former state, see what we shall do to them." And his hosts said to him, "Power is thine, O our Yahuah, to do what you list."
Then Satan, great in wickedness, took his hosts and came into the cave, in the thirtieth night of the forty days and one; and he beat Adawm and Ḥawwah, until he left them dead.
Then came the Word of Aluahim to Adawm and Ḥawwah, who raised them from their suffering, and Aluahim said to Adawm, "Be strong, and be not afraid of him who has just come to you."
But Adawm cried and said, "Where were you, O my Aluahim, that they should punish me with such blows, and that this suffering should come over us; over me and over Ḥawwah, Your handmaiden?"
Then Aluahim said to him, "O Adawm, see, he is Yahuah and master of all you have, he who said, he would give you divinity. Where is this love for you? And where is the gift he promised?

Did it please him just once, O Adawm, to come to you, comfort you, strengthen you, rejoice with you, or send his hosts to protect you; because you have obeyed him, and have yielded to his counsel; and have followed his mitsvah and transgressed Mine?"

Then Adawm cried before Yahuah, and said, "O Yahuah because I transgressed a little, You have severely punished me in return for it, I ask You to deliver me out of his hands; or Alse have pity on me, and take my nephesh out of my body now in this strange land."
Then Aluahim said to Adawm, "If only there had been this sighing and praying before, before you transgressed! Then would you have rest from the trouble in which you are now."
But Aluahim had patience with Adawm, and let him and Ḥawwah remain in the cave until they had fulfilled the forty days.

But as to Adawm and Ḥawwah, their strength and flesh withered from fasting and praying, from hunger and thirst; for they had not tasted either food or drink since they left the garden; nor were the functions of their bodies yet settled; and they had no strength left to continue in prayer from hunger, until the end of the next day to the fortieth. They were fallen down in the cave; yet what speech escaped from their mouths, was only in praises.

Then on the eighty−ninth day, Satan came to the cave, clad in a garment of light, and girt about with a bright girdle. First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter LIXEighth apparition of Satan of Satan to Adawm and Ḥawwah. 42
In his hands was a staff of light, and he looked most awful; but his face was pleasant and his speech was sweet.
He thus transformed himself in order to deceive Adawm and Ḥawwah, and to make them come out of the cave, before they had fulfilled the forty days.

For he said within himself, "Now that when they had fulfilled the forty days' fasting and praying, Aluahim would restore them to their former state; but if He did not do so, He would still be favorable to them; and even if He had not mercy on them, would He yet give them something from the garden to comfort them; as already twice before."
Then Satan drew near the cave in this fair appearance, and said: −−
"O Adawm, get up, stand up, you and Ḥawwah, and come along with me, to a good land; and don't be afraid. I am flesh and bones like you; and at first I was a creature that Aluahim created.
And it was so, that when He had created me, He placed me in a garden in the north, on the border of the world.
And He said to me, 'Stay here!' And I remained there according to His Word, neither did I transgress His mitsvah.
Then He made a slumber to come over me, and He brought you, O Adawm, out of my side, but did not make you stay with me.
But Aluahim took you in His divine hand, and placed you in a garden to the eastward.
Then I worried about you, for that while Aluahim had taken you out of my side, He had not let you stay with me.
But Aluahim said to me: 'Do not worry about Adawm, whom I brought out of your side; no harm will come to him.
For now I have brought out of his side a help−meet* for him; and I have given him joy by so doing.' "

Then Satan said again, "I did not know how it is you are in this cave, nor anything about this trial that has come over you −− until Aluahim said to me, 'Behold, Adawm has transgressed, he whom I had taken out of your side, and Ḥawwah also, whom I took out of his side; and I have driven them out of the garden; I have made them live in a land of sorrow and misery, because they transgressed against Me, and have obeyed Satan. And look, they are in suffering until this day, the eightieth.' 15 Then Aluahim said to me, 'Get up, go to them, and make them come to your place, and suffer not that Satan come near them, and afflict them. For they are now in great misery; and lie helpless from hunger.'

He further said to me, 'When you have taken them to yourself, give them to eat of the fruit of the Tree of Life, and give them to drink of the water of shalom; and clothe them in a garment of light, and restore them to their former state of chen, and leave them not in misery, for they came from you. But grieve not over them, nor repent of that which has come over them.
But when I heard this, I was sorry; and my heart could not patiently bear it for your sake, O my child.
But, O Adawm, when I heard the name of Satan, I was afraid, and I said within myself, I will not come out because he might trap me as he did my children, Adawm and Ḥawwah.

And I said, 'O Aluahim, when I go to my children, Satan will meet me in the way, and war against me, as he did against them.' First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter LIXEighth apparition of Satan of Satan to Adawm and Ḥawwah. 43
Then Aluahim said to me, 'Fear not; when you find him, hit him with the staff that is in thine hand, and don't be afraid of him, for you are of old standing, and he shall not prevail against you.'
Then I said, 'O my Yahuah, I am old, and cannot go. Send Your mal'akim to bring them.'

But Aluahim said to me, 'Mal'akim, verily, are not like them; and they will not consent to come with them. But I have chosen you, because they are your offspring and are like you, and they will listen to what you say.'

Aluahim said further to me, 'If you don't have enough strength to walk, I will send a cloud to carry you and set you down at the entrance of their cave; then the cloud will return and leave you there.
And if they will come with you, I will send a cloud to carry you and them.'
Then He commanded a cloud, and it bear me up and brought me to you; and then went back.
And now, O my children, Adawm and Ḥawwah, look at my old gray hair and at my feeble state, and at my coming from that distant place. Come, come with me, to a place of rest."
Then he began to cry and to sob before Adawm and Ḥawwah, and his tears poured on the ground like water.
And when Adawm and Ḥawwah raised their eyes and saw his beard, and heard his sweet talk, their hearts softened towards him; they obeyed him, for they believed he was true.

And it seemed to them that they were really his offspring, when they saw that his face was like their own; and they trusted him. * The existence of the two words helpmeet and helpmate, meaning exactly the same thing, is a comedy of errors. Aluahim's promise to Adawm, as rendered in the King James version of the Bible, was to give him an help meet for him (that is, a helper fit for him). In the 17th century the two words help and meet in this passage were mistaken for one word, applying to Ḥawwah, and thus helpmeet came to mean a wife. Then in the 18th century, in a misguided attempt to make sense of the word, the spelling helpmate was introduced. Both errors are now beyond recall, and both spellings are acceptable.
Then he took Adawm and Ḥawwah by the hand, and began to bring them out of the cave.

But when they had come a little ways out of it, Aluahim knew that Satan had overcome them, and had brought them out before the forty days were ended, to take them to some distant place, and to destroy them.
Then the Word of Yahuah Aluahim again came and cursed Satan, and drove him away from them.
And Aluahim began to speak to Adawm and Ḥawwah, saying to them, "What made you come out of the cave, to this place?"

Then Adawm said to Aluahim, "Did you create a man before us? For when we were in the cave there suddenly came to us a friendly old man who said to us, 'I am a mal'ak from Aluahim to you, to bring you back to some place of rest.'

And we believed, O Aluahim, that he was a mal'ak from you; and we came out with him; and knew not where we should go with him." First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter LXIThey begin to follow Satan. 44

Then Aluahim said to Adawm, "See, that is the father of evil arts, who brought you and Ḥawwah out of the Garden of Delights. And now, indeed, when he saw that you and Ḥawwah both joined together in fasting and praying, and that you came not out of the cave before the end of the forty days, he wished to make your purpose vein, to break your mutual bond; to cut off all hope from you, and to drive you to some place where he might destroy you.
Because he couldn't do anything to you unless he showed himself in the likeness of you.
Therefore he came to you with a face like your own, and began to give you tokens as if they were all true.
But because I am merciful and am favorable to you, I did not allow him to destroy you; instead I drove him away from you.
Now, therefore, O Adawm, take Ḥawwah, and return to your cave, and remain in it until the morning after the fortieth day. And when you come out, go towards the eastern gate of the garden."

Then Adawm and Ḥawwah worshipped Aluahim, and praised and baruk Him for the deliverance that had come to them from Him. And they returned towards the cave. This happened in the evening of the thirty−ninth day.

Then Adawm and Ḥawwah stood up and with a fiery passion, prayed to Aluahim, to give them strength; for they had become weak because of hunger and thirst and prayer. But they watched the whole of that night praying, until morning.
Then Adawm said to Ḥawwah, "Get up, let us go towards the eastern gate of the garden as Aluahim told us."
And they said their prayers as they were accustomed to do every day; and they left the cave to go near to the eastern gate of the garden.
Then Adawm and Ḥawwah stood up and prayed, and appealed to Aluahim to strengthen them, and to send them something to satisfy their hunger.
But after they finished their prayers, they were too weak to move.
Then came the Word of Aluahim again, and said to them, "O Adawm, get up, go and bring the two figs here."
Then Adawm and Ḥawwah got up, and went until they came near to the cave.
But Satan the wicked was envious, because of the consolation Aluahim had given them.
So he prevented them, and went into the cave and took the two figs, and buried them outside the cave, so that Adawm and Ḥawwah should not find them. He also had in his thoughts to destroy them.

But by Aluahim's mercy, as soon as those two figs were in the ground, Aluahim defeated Satan's counsel regarding them; and made them into two fruit trees, that overshadowed the cave. For Satan had buried them on the eastern side of it.

Then when the two trees were grown, and were covered with fruit, Satan grieved and mourned, and said, "It would have been better to have left those figs where they were; for now, behold, they have become two fruit trees, whereof Adawm will eat all the days of his life. Whereas I had in mind, when I buried them, to destroy them First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter LXIITwo fruit trees. 45 entirely, and to hide them forever.
But Aluahim has overturned my counsel; and would not that this sacred fruit should perish; and He has made plain my intention, and has defeated the counsel I had formed against His servants."
Then Satan went away ashamed because he hadn't thought his plans all the way through.
But Adawm and Ḥawwah, as they got closer to the cave, saw two fig trees, covered with fruit, and overshadowing the cave.

Then Adawm said to Ḥawwah, "It seems to me that we have gone the wrong way. When did these two trees grow here? It seems to me that the enemy wishes to lead us the wrong way. Do you suppose that there is another cave besides this one in the earth?
Yet, O Ḥawwah, let us go into the cave, and find in it the two figs; for this is our cave, in which we were. But if we should not find the two figs in it, then it cannot be our cave."
They went then into the cave, and looked into the four corners of it, but found not the two figs.

And Adawm cried and said to Ḥawwah, "Did we go to the wrong cave, then, O Ḥawwah? It seems to me these two fig trees are the two figs that were in the cave." And Ḥawwah said, "I, for my part, do not know."
Then Adawm stood up and prayed and said, "O Aluahim, You commanded us to come back to the cave, to take the two figs, and then to return to you.

But now, we have not found them. O Aluahim, have you taken them, and sown these two trees, or have we gone astray in the earth; or has the enemy deceived us? If it be real, then, O Aluahim, reveal to us the secret of these two trees and of the two figs."

Then came the Word of Aluahim to Adawm, and said to him, "O Adawm, when I sent you to fetch the figs, Satan went before you to the cave, took the figs, and buried them outside, eastward of the cave, thinking to destroy them; and not sowing them with good intent.

Not for his mere sake, then, have these trees grown up at once; but I had mercy on you and I commanded them to grow. And they grew to be two large trees, that you be overshadowed by their branches, and find rest; and that I made you see My power and My marvelous works.

And, also, to show you Satan's meanness, and his evil works, for ever since you came out of the garden, he has not ceased, no, not one day, from doing you some harm. But I have not given him power over you."
And Aluahim said, "From now on, O Adawm, rejoice on account of the trees, you and Ḥawwah; and rest under them when you feel weary. But do not eat any of their fruit or come near them."
Then Adawm cried, and said, "O Aluahim, will You again kill us, or will You drive us away from before Your face, and cut our life from off the face of the earth?

O Aluahim, I beg you, if You know that there be in these trees either death or some other evil, as at the first time, root them up from near our cave, and with them; and leave us to die of the heat, of hunger and of thirst. First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter LXIIIThe first joy of trees. 46

For we know Your marvelous works, O Aluahim, that they are great, and that by Your power You can bring one thing out of another, without one's wish. For Your power can make rocks to become trees, and trees to become rocks."

Then Aluahim looked at Adawm and at his strength of mind, at his endurance of hunger and thirst, and of the heat. And He changed the two fig trees into two figs, as they were at first, and then said to Adawm and to Ḥawwah, "Each of you may take one fig." And they took them, as Yahuah commanded them.
And He said to them, "You must now go into the cave and eat the figs, and satisfy your hunger, or Alse you will die."
So, as Aluahim commanded them, they went into the cave about sunset. And Adawm and Ḥawwah stood up and prayed during the setting sun.

Then they sat down to eat the figs; but they knew not how to eat them; for they were not accustomed to eat earthly food. They were afraid that if they ate, their stomach would be burdened and their flesh thickened, and their hearts would take to liking earthly food.
But while they were thus seated, Aluahim, out of pity for them, sent them His mal'ak, so they wouldn't perish of hunger and thirst.

And the mal'ak said to Adawm and Ḥawwah, "Aluahim says to you that you do not have the strength that would be required to fast until death; eat, therefore, and strengthen your bodies; for you are now animal flesh and cannot subsist without food and drink."
Then Adawm and Ḥawwah took the figs and began to eat of them. But Aluahim had put into them a mixture as of savory bread and blood.

Then the mal'ak went from Adawm and Ḥawwah, who ate of the figs until they had satisfied their hunger. Then they put aside what was left; but by the power of Aluahim, the figs became whole again, because Aluahim baruk them.
After this Adawm and Ḥawwah got up, and prayed with a joyful heart and renewed strength, and praised and rejoiced abundantly the whole of that night. And this was the end of the eighty−third day.
And when it was day, they got up and prayed, after their custom, and then went out of the cave.
But they became sick from the food they had eaten because they were not used to it, so they went about in the cave saying to each other: −−

"What has our eating caused to happen to us, that we should be in such pain? We are in misery, we shall die! It would have been better for us to have died keeping our bodies pure than to have eaten and defiled them with food."

Then Adawm said to Ḥawwah, "This pain did not come to us in the garden, neither did we eat such bad food there. Do you think, O Ḥawwah, that Aluahim will plague us through the food that is in us, or that our innards will come out; or that First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter LXIVAdawm and Ḥawwah partake of the first earthly food. 47 Aluahim means to kill us with this pain before He has fulfilled His promise to us?"

Then Adawm besought Yahuah and said, "O Yahuah, let us not perish through the food we have eaten. O Yahuah, don't punish us; but deal with us according to Your great mercy, and forsake us not until the day of the promise You have made us."
Then Aluahim looked at them, and then fitted them for eating food at once; as to this day; so that they should not perish.

Then Adawm and Ḥawwah came back into the cave sorrowful and crying because of the alteration of their bodies. And they both knew from that hour that they were altered beings, that all hope of returning to the garden was now lost; and that they could not enter it.
For that now their bodies had strange functions; and all flesh that requires food and drink for its existence, cannot be in the garden.

Then Adawm said to Ḥawwah, "Behold, our hope is now lost; and so is our trust to enter the garden. We no longer belong to the inhabitants of the garden; but from now on we are earthy and of the dust, and of the inhabitants of the earth. We shall not return to the garden, until the day in which Aluahim has promised to save us, and to bring us again into the garden, as He promised us."

Then they prayed to Aluahim that He would have mercy on them; after which, their mind was quieted, their hearts were broken, and their longing was cooled down; and they were like strangers on earth. That night Adawm and Ḥawwah spent in the cave, where they slept heavily by reason of the food they had eaten.

When it was morning, the day after they had eaten food, Adawm and Ḥawwah prayed in the cave, and Adawm said to Ḥawwah, "Look, we asked for food of Aluahim, and He gave it. But now let us also ask Him to give us a drink of water."

Then they got up, and went to the bank of the stream of water, that was on the south border of the garden, in which they had before thrown themselves. And they stood on the bank, and prayed to Aluahim that He would command them to drink of the water.
Then the Word of Aluahim came to Adawm, and said to him, "O Adawm, your body has become brutish, and requires water to drink. Take some and drink it, you and Ḥawwah, then give thanks and praise."

Adawm and Ḥawwah then went down to the stream and drank from it, until their bodies felt refreshed. After having drunk, they praised Aluahim, and then returned to their cave, after their former custom. This happened at the end of eighty−three days.

Then on the eighty−fourth day, they took the two figs and hung them in the cave, together with the leaves thereof, to be to them a sign and a berakah from Aluahim. And they placed them there so that if their descendants came there, they would see the wonderful things Aluahim had done for them.
Then Adawm and Ḥawwah again stood outside the cave, and asked Aluahim to show them some food with which they could nourish their bodies.

Then the Word of Aluahim came and said to him, "O Adawm, go down to the westward of the cave until you come to a land of dark soil, and there you shall find food." First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter LXVIAdawm does his first day's work. 48
And Adawm obeyed the Word of Aluahim, took Ḥawwah, and went down to a land of dark soil, and found there wheat* growing in the ear and ripe, and figs to eat; and Adawm rejoiced over it.

Then the Word of Aluahim came again to Adawm, and said to him, "Take some of this wheat and make yourselves some bread with it, to nourish your body therewith." And Aluahim gave Adawm's heart wisdom, to work out the corn until it became bread.

Adawm accomplished all that, until he grew very faint and weary. He then returned to the cave; rejoicing at what he had learned of what is done with wheat, until it is made into bread for one's use. * In this book, the terms 'corn' and 'wheat' are used interchangeably. The reference is possibly used to indicate a type of ancient grain resembling Mitsrite Corn also known as Durra. Durra is a wheat−like cereal grain frequently cultivated in dry regions such as Mitsrayim.

When Adawm and Ḥawwah went down to the land of black mud and came near to the wheat Aluahim had showed them and saw that it was ripe and ready for reaping, they did not have a sickle to reap it with. So they readied themselves, and began to pull up the wheat by hand, until it was all done.
Then they heaped it into a pile; and, faint from heat and from thirst, they went under a shady tree, where the breeze fanned them to sleep.

But Satan saw what Adawm and Ḥawwah had done. And he called his hosts, and said to them, "Since Aluahim has shown to Adawm and Ḥawwah all about this wheat, wherewith to strengthen their bodies −− and, look, they have come and made a big pile of it, and faint from the toil are now asleep −− come, let us set fire to this heap of corn, and burn it, and let us take that bottle of water that is by them, and empty it out, so that they may find nothing to drink, and we kill them with hunger and thirst.

Then, when they wake up from their sleep, and seek to return to the cave, we will come to them in the way, and will lead them astray; so that they die of hunger and thirst; when they may, perhaps, deny Aluahim, and He destroy them. So shall we be rid of them."
Then Satan and his hosts set the wheat on fire and burned it up.
But from the heat of the flame Adawm and Ḥawwah awoke from their sleep, and saw the wheat burning, and the bucket of water by them, poured out.
Then they cried and went back to the cave.
But as they were going up from below the mountain where they were, Satan and his hosts met them in the form of mal'akim, praising Aluahim.
Then Satan said to Adawm, "O Adawm, why are you so pained with hunger and thirst? It seems to me that Satan has burnt up the wheat." And Adawm said to him, "Yes."

Again Satan said to Adawm, "Come back with us; we are mal'akim of Aluahim. Aluahim sent us to you, to show you another field of corn, better than that; and beyond it is a fountain of good water, and many trees, where you shall live near it, and work the corn field to better purpose than that which Satan has consumed."

Adawm thought that he was true, and that they were mal'akim who talked with him; and he went back with them. First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter LXVII"Then Satan began to lead astray Adawm and Ḥawwah. . . ." 49
Then Satan began to lead astray Adawm and Ḥawwah eight days, until they both fell down as if dead, from hunger, thirst, and faintness. Then he fled with his hosts, and left them.
Then Aluahim looked at Adawm and Ḥawwah, and at what had come over them from Satan, and how he had made them perish.
Aluahim, therefore, sent His Word, and raised up Adawm and Ḥawwah from their state of death.

Then, Adawm, when he was raised, said, "O Aluahim, You have burnt and taken from us the corn You have given us, and You have emptied out the bucket of water. And You have sent Your mal'akim, who have caused us to lose our way from the corn field. Will You make us perish? If this be from you, O Aluahim, then take away our nepheshoth; but punish us not."
Then Aluahim said to Adawm, "I did not burn down the wheat, and I did not pour the water out of the bucket, and I did not send My mal'akim to lead you astray.

But it is Satan, your master who did it; he to whom you have subjected yourself; my mitsvah being meanwhile set aside. He it is, who burnt down the corn, and poured out the water, and who has led you astray; and all the promises he has made you were just a trick, a deception, and a lie.
But now, O Adawm, you shall acknowledge My good deeds done to you."

And Aluahim told His mal'akim to take Adawm and Ḥawwah, and to bear them up to the field of wheat, which they found as before, with the bucket full of water. 8 There they saw a tree, and found on it solid manna; and wondered at Aluahim's power. And the mal'akim commanded them to eat of the manna when they were hungry.
And Aluahim admonished Satan with a curse, not to come again, and destroy the field of corn.
Then Adawm and Ḥawwah took of the corn, and made of it an offering, and took it and offered it up on the mountain, the place where they had offered up their first offering of blood.

And they offered this offering again on the mizbe'ach they had built at first. And they stood up and prayed, and besought Yahuah saying, "Thus, O Aluahim, when we were in the garden, our praises went up to you, like this offering; and our innocence went up to you like incense. But now, O Aluahim, accept this offering from us, and don't turn us away, deprived of Your mercy."

Then Aluahim said to Adawm and Ḥawwah, "Since you have made this offering and have offered it to Me, I shall make it My flesh, when I come down on earth to save you; and I shall cause it to be offered continually on an mizbe'ach, for forgiveness and for mercy, for those who partake of it duly."
And Aluahim sent a bright fire over the offering of Adawm and Ḥawwah, and filled it with brightness, chen, and light; and the Ruach haQodesh came down on that offering.

Then Aluahim commanded an mal'ak to take fire tongs, like a spoon, and with it to take an offering and bring it to Adawm and Ḥawwah. And the mal'ak did so, as Aluahim had commanded him, and offered it to them. First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter LXVIIIHow destruction and trouble is of Satan when he is the master. Adawm and Ḥawwah establish the custom of worship.50
And the nepheshoth of Adawm and Ḥawwah were brightened, and their hearts were filled with joy and gladness and with the praises of Aluahim.

And Aluahim said to Adawm, "This shall be to you a custom, to do so, when affliction and sorrow come over you. But your deliverance and your entrance in to the garden, shall not be until the days are fulfilled as agreed between you and Me; were it not so, I would, of My mercy and pity for you, bring you back to My garden and to My chen for the sake of the offering you have just made to My name."
Adawm rejoiced at these words which he heard from Aluahim; and he and Ḥawwah worshipped before the mizbe'ach, to which they bowed, and then went back to the Cave of Treasures.
And this took place at the end of the twelfth day after the eightieth day, from the time Adawm and Ḥawwah came out of the garden.
And they stood up the whole night praying until morning; and then went out of the cave.

Then Adawm said to Ḥawwah, with joy of heart, because of the offering they had made to Aluahim, and that had been accepted of Him, "Let us do this three times every week, on the fourth day Wednesday, on the preparation day Friday, and on the Shabbath Sunday, all the days of our life."
And as they agreed to these words between themselves, Aluahim was pleased with their thoughts, and with the resolution they had each taken with the other.

After this, came the Word of Aluahim to Adawm, and said, "O Adawm, you have determined beforehand the days in which sufferings shall come over Me, when I am made flesh; for they are the fourth Wednesday, and the preparation day Friday.

But as to the first day, I created in it all things, and I raised the shamayim. And, again, through My rising again on this day, will I create joy, and raise them on high, who believe in Me; O Adawm, offer this offering, all the days of your life."
Then Aluahim withdrew His Word from Adawm.

But Adawm continued to offer this offering thus, every week three times, until the end of seven weeks. And on the first day, which is the fiftieth, Adawm made an offering as he was accustomed, and he and Ḥawwah took it and came to the mizbe'ach before Aluahim, as He had taught them.

Then Satan, the hater of all good, envious of Adawm and of his offering through which he found chen with Aluahim, hastened and took a sharp stone from among the sharp iron stones; appeared in the form of a man, and went and stood by Adawm and Ḥawwah.
Adawm was then offering on the mizbe'ach, and had begun to pray, with his hands spread before Aluahim.

Then Satan hastened with the sharp iron stone he had with him, and with it pierced Adawm on the right side, from which flowed blood and water, then Adawm fell on the mizbe'ach like a corpse. And Satan fled. First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter LXIXTwelfth apparition of Satan to Adawm and Ḥawwah, while Adawm was praying over the offering on the mizbe'ach; when Satan beat him.51
Then Ḥawwah came, and took Adawm and placed him below the mizbe'ach. And there she stayed, crying over him; while a stream of blood flowed from Adawm's side over his offering.
But Aluahim looked at the death of Adawm. He then sent His Word, and raised him up and said to him, "Fulfil your offering, for indeed, Adawm, it is worth much, and there is no shortcoming in it."

Aluahim said further to Adawm, "Thus will it also happen to Me, on the earth, when I shall be pierced and blood and water shall flow from My side and run over My body, which is the true offering; and which shall be offered on the mizbe'ach as a perfect offering."
Then Aluahim commanded Adawm to finish his offering, and when he had ended it he worshipped before Aluahim, and praised Him for the signs He had showed him.
And Aluahim healed Adawm in one day, which is the end of the seven weeks; and that is the fiftieth day.

Then Adawm and Ḥawwah returned from the mountain, and went into the Cave of Treasures, as they were used to do. This completed for Adawm and Ḥawwah, one hundred and forty days since their coming out of the garden.

Then they both stood up that night and prayed to Aluahim. And when it was morning, they went out, and went down westward of the cave, to the place where their corn was, and there rested under the shadow of a tree, as they were accustomed.
But when there a multitude of beasts came all around them. It was Satan's doing, in his wickedness; in order to wage war against Adawm through marriage.
After this Satan, the hater of all good, took the form of an mal'ak, and with him two others, so that they looked like the three mal'akim who had brought to Adawm gold, incense, and myrrh.
They passed before Adawm and Ḥawwah while they were under the tree, and greeted Adawm and Ḥawwah with fair words that were full of deceit.

But when Adawm and Ḥawwah saw their pleasant expression, and heard their sweet speech, Adawm rose, welcomed them, and brought them to Ḥawwah, and they remained all together; Adawm's heart the while, being glad because he thought concerning them, that they were the same mal'akim, who had brought him gold, incense, and myrrh.

Because, when they came to Adawm the first time, there came over him from them, shalom and joy, through their bringing him good tokens; so Adawm thought that they had come a second time to give him other tokens for him to rejoice therewith. For he did not know it was Satan; therefore he received them with joy and consorted with them.
Then Satan, the tallest of them, said, "Rejoice, O Adawm, and be glad. Look, Aluahim has sent us to you to tell you something."

And Adawm said, "What is it?" Then Satan answered, "It is a simple thing, yet it is the Word of Aluahim, will you accept it from us and do it? But if you will not accept it, we will return to Aluahim, and tell Him that you would not receive His Word."

And Satan said again to Adawm, "Don't be afraid and don't tremble; don't you know us?" First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter LXXThirteenth apparition of Satan, to trick Adawm into marrying Ḥawwah. 52
But Adawm said, "I do not know you."

Then Satan said to him, "I am the mal'ak that brought you gold, and took it to the cave; this other mal'ak is the one that brought you incense; and that third mal'ak, is the one who brought you myrrh when you were on top of the mountain, and who carried you to the cave. 10 But as to the other mal'akim our fellows, who bare you to the cave, Aluahim has not sent them with us this time; for He said to us, 'You will be enough'. "
So when Adawm heard these words he believed them, and said to these mal'akim, "Speak the Word of Aluahim, that I may receive it."
And Satan said to him, "Swear, and promise me that you will receive it."
Then Adawm said, "I do not know how to swear and promise."
And Satan said to him, "Hold out your hand, and put it inside my hand."

Then Adawm held out his hand, and put it into Satan's hand; when Satan said to him, "Say, now −− So true as Aluahim is living, rational, and speaking, who raised the stars in shamayim, and established the dry ground on the waters, and has created me out of the four Alements*, and out of the dust of the earth −− I will not break my promise, nor renounce my word."
And Adawm swore thus.

Then Satan said to him, "Look, it is now some time since you came out of the garden, and you know neither wickedness nor evil. But now Aluahim says to you, to take Ḥawwah who came out of your side, and to marry her so that she will bear you children, to comfort you, and to drive from you trouble and sorrow; now this thing is not difficult, neither is there any scandal in it to you. * See the previous footnote in Chapter XXXIV regarding the 'four Alements'.

But when Adawm heard these words from Satan, he sorrowed much, because of his oath and of his promise, and said, "Shall I commit adultery with my flesh and my bones, and shall I sin against myself, for Aluahim to destroy me, and to blot me out from off the face of the earth?

Since, when at first, I ate of the tree, He drove me out of the garden into this strange land, and deprived me of my bright nature, and brought death over me. If, then, I should do this, He will cut off my life from the earth, and He will cast me into hell, and will plague me there a long time. 3 But Aluahim never spoke the words that you have said; and you are not Aluahim's mal'akim, and you weren't sent from Him. But you are devils that have come to me under the false appearance of mal'akim. Away from me; you cursed of Aluahim!"
Then those devils fled from before Adawm. And he and Ḥawwah got up, and returned to the Cave of Treasures, and went into it.

Then Adawm said to Ḥawwah, "If you saw what I did, don't tell anyone; for I sinned against Aluahim in swearing by His great name, and I have placed my hand another time into that of Satan." Ḥawwah, then, held her shalom, as Adawm told her.

Then Adawm got up, and spread his hands before Aluahim, beseeching and entreating Him with tears, to forgive him what he had done. And Adawm remained thus standing and praying forty days and forty nights. He neither ate nor First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter LXXIAdawm is troubled by the thought of marrying Ḥawwah. 53 drank until he dropped down on the ground from hunger and thirst.
Then Aluahim sent His Word to Adawm, who raised him up from where he lay, and said to him, "O Adawm, why have you sworn by My name, and why have you made agreement with Satan another time?"
But Adawm cried, and said, "O Aluahim, forgive me, for I did this unwittingly; believing they were Aluahim's mal'akim."
And Aluahim forgave Adawm, saying to him, "Beware of Satan."
And He withdrew His Word from Adawm.
Then Adawm's heart was comforted; and he took Ḥawwah, and they went out of the cave, to prepare some food for their bodies.
But from that day Adawm struggled in his mind about his marrying Ḥawwah; afraid that if he was to do it, Aluahim would be angry with him.
Then Adawm and Ḥawwah went to the river of water, and sat on the bank, as people do when they enjoy themselves.
But Satan was jealous of them; and planned to destroy them.
Then Satan, and ten from his hosts, transformed themselves into maidens, unlike any others in the whole world for chen.

They came up out of the river in presence of Adawm and Ḥawwah, and they said among themselves, "Come, we will look at the faces of Adawm and Ḥawwah, who are of the men on earth. How beautiful they are, and how different is their look from our own faces." Then they came to Adawm and Ḥawwah, and greeted them; and stood wondering at them.
Adawm and Ḥawwah looked at them also, and wondered at their beauty, and said, "Is there, then, under us, another world, with such beautiful creatures as these in it?"
And those maidens said to Adawm and Ḥawwah, "Yes, indeed, we are an abundant creation."
Then Adawm said to them, "But how do you multiply?"

And they answered him, "We have husbands who have married us, and we bear them children, who grow up, and who in their turn marry and are married, and also bear children; and thus we increase. And if so be, O Adawm, you will not believe us, we will show you our husbands and our children."
Then they shouted over the river as if to call their husbands and their children, who came up from the river, men and children; and every man came to his wife, his children being with him.
But when Adawm and Ḥawwah saw them, they stood dumb, and wondered at them.

Then they said to Adawm and Ḥawwah, "See all our husbands and our children? You should marry Ḥawwah, as we have married our husbands, so that you will have children as we have." This was a device of Satan to deceive Adawm. First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter LXXIIAdawm's heart is set on fire. Satan appears as beautiful maidens. 54

Satan also thought within himself, "Aluahim at first commanded Adawm concerning the fruit of the tree, saying to him, 'Eat not of it; Alse of death you shall die.' But Adawm ate of it, and yet Aluahim did not kill him; He only decreed on him death, and plagues and trials, until the day he shall come out of his body.
Now, then, if I deceive him to do this thing, and to marry Ḥawwah without Aluahim's permission, Aluahim will kill him then."
Therefore Satan worked this apparition before Adawm and Ḥawwah; because he sought to kill him, and to make him disappear from off the face of the earth.
Meanwhile the fire of sin came over Adawm, and he thought of committing sin. But he restrained himself, fearing that if he followed this advice of Satan, Aluahim would put him to death.
Then Adawm and Ḥawwah got up, and prayed to Aluahim, while Satan and his hosts went down into the river, in presence of Adawm and Ḥawwah; to let them see that they were going back to their own world.
Then Adawm and Ḥawwah went back to the Cave of Treasures, as they usually did; about evening time.

And they both got up and prayed to Aluahim that night. Adawm remained standing in prayer, yet not knowing how to pray, by reason of the thoughts in his heart regarding his marrying Ḥawwah; and he continued so until morning.
And when light came up, Adawm said to Ḥawwah, "Get up, let us go below the mountain, where they brought us gold, and let us ask Yahuah concerning this matter."
Then Ḥawwah said, "What is that matter, O Adawm?"

And he answered her, "That I may request Yahuah to inform me about marrying you; for I will not do it without His permission or Alse He will make us perish, you and me. For those devils have set my heart on fire, with thoughts of what they showed us, in their sinful apparitions.
Then Ḥawwah said to Adawm, "Why need we go below the mountain? Let us rather stand up and pray in our cave to Aluahim, to let us know whether this counsel is good or not."

Then Adawm rose up in prayer and said, "O Aluahim, you know that we transgressed against you, and from the moment we transgressed, we were stripped of our bright nature; and our body became brutish, requiring food and drink; and with animal desires.

Command us, O Aluahim, not to give way to them without Your permission, for fear that You will turn us into nothing. Because if you do not give us permission, we shall be overpowered, and follow that advice of Satan; and You will again make us perish.

If not, then take our nepheshoth from us; let us be rid of this animal lust. And if You give us no order respecting this thing, then sever Ḥawwah from me, and me from her; and place us each far away from the other.

Then again, O Aluahim, if You separate us from each other, the devils will deceive us with their apparitions that resemble us, and destroy our hearts, and defile our thoughts towards each other. Yet if it is not each of us towards the other, it will, at all events, be through their appearance when the devils come to us in our likeness." Here Adawm ended his prayer. First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter LXXIIAdawm's heart is set on fire. Satan appears as beautiful maidens. 55
Then Aluahim considered the words of Adawm that they were true, and that he could long await His order, respecting the counsel of Satan.

And Aluahim approved Adawm in what he had thought concerning this, and in the prayer he had offered in His presence; and the Word of Aluahim came to Adawm and said to him, "O Adawm, if only you had had this caution at first, before you came out of the garden into this land!"

After that, Aluahim sent His mal'ak who had brought gold, and the mal'ak who had brought incense, and the mal'ak who had brought myrrh to Adawm, that they should inform him respecting his marriage to Ḥawwah.

Then those mal'akim said to Adawm, "Take the gold and give it to Ḥawwah as a wedding gift, and promise to marry her; then give her some incense and myrrh as a present; and be you, you and she, one flesh."
Adawm obeyed the mal'akim, and took the gold and put it into Ḥawwah's bosom in her garment; and promised to marry her with his hand.

Then the mal'akim commanded Adawm and Ḥawwah to get up and pray forty days and forty nights; when that was done, then Adawm was to have sexual intercourse with his wife; for then this would be an act pure and undefiled; so that he would have children who would multiply, and replenish the face of the earth.
Then both Adawm and Ḥawwah received the words of the mal'akim; and the mal'akim departed from them.

Then Adawm and Ḥawwah began to fast and pray, until the end of the forty days; and then they had sexual intercourse, as the mal'akim had told them. And from the time Adawm left the garden until he wedded Ḥawwah, were two hundred and twenty−three days, that is seven months and thirteen days.
Thus was Satan's war with Adawm defeated.

And they lived on the earth working in order to keep their bodies in good health; and they continued so until the nine months of Ḥawwah's pregnancy were over, and the time drew near when she must give birth.

Then she said to Adawm, "The signs placed in this cave since we left the garden indicate that this is a pure place and we will be praying in it again some time. It is not appropriate then, that I should give birth in it. Let us instead go to the sheltering rock cave that was formed by the command of Aluahim when Satan threw a big rock down on us in an attempt to kill us with it.

Adawm then took Ḥawwah to that cave. When the time came for her to give birth, she strained a lot. Adawm felt sorry, and he was very worried about her because she was close to death and the words of Aluahim to her were being fulfilled: "In suffering shall you bear a child, and in sorrow shall you bring forth a child."
But when Adawm saw the distress in which Ḥawwah was, he got up and prayed to Aluahim, and said, "O Yahuah, look at me with the eye of Your mercy, and bring her out of her distress."

And Aluahim looked at His maid−servant Ḥawwah, and delivered her, and she gave birth to her first−born son, and with him a daughter. First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter LXXIIIThe marriage of Adawm and Ḥawwah. 56

The Adawm rejoiced at Ḥawwah's deliverance, and also over the children she had borne him. And Adawm ministered to Ḥawwah in the cave, until the end of eight days; when they named the son Qayin, and the daughter Luluwa.
The meaning of Qayin is "hater," because he hated his sister in their mother's womb; before they came out of it. Therefore Adawm named him Qayin.
But Luluwa means "beautiful," because she was more beautiful than her mother.
Then Adawm and Ḥawwah waited until Qayin and his sister were forty days old, when Adawm said to Ḥawwah, "We will make an offering and offer it up in behalf of the children."
And Ḥawwah said, "We will make one offering for the first−born son and then later we shall make one for the daughter."
Then Adawm prepared an offering, and he and Ḥawwah offered it up for their children, and brought it to the mizbe'ach they had built at first.
And Adawm offered up the offering, and asked Aluahim to accept his offering.
Then Aluahim accepted Adawm's offering, and sent a light from shamayim that shown on the offering. Adawm and his son drew near to the offering, but Ḥawwah and the daughter did not approach it.

Adawm and his son were joyful as they came down from on the mizbe'ach. Adawm and Ḥawwah waited until the daughter was eighty days old, then Adawm prepared an offering and took it to Ḥawwah and to the children. They went to the mizbe'ach, where Adawm offered it up, as he was accustomed, asking Yahuah to accept his offering.
And Yahuah accepted the offering of Adawm and Ḥawwah. Then Adawm, Ḥawwah, and the children, drew near together, and came down from the mountain, rejoicing.
But they returned not to the cave in which they were born; but came to the Cave of Treasures, in order that the children should go around in it, and be baruk with the tokens brought from the garden.
But after they had been baruk with these tokens, they went back to the cave in which they were born.

However, before Ḥawwah had offered up the offering, Adawm had taken her, and had gone with her to the river of water, in which they threw themselves at first; and there they washed themselves. Adawm washed his body and Ḥawwah hers also clean, after the suffering and distress that had come over them.

But Adawm and Ḥawwah, after washing themselves in the river of water, returned every night to the Cave of Treasures, where they prayed and were baruk; and then went back to their cave, where their children were born.

Adawm and Ḥawwah did this until the children had been weaned. After they were weaned, Adawm made an offering for the nepheshoth of his children in addition to the three times every week he made an offering for them.

When the children were weaned, Ḥawwah again conceived, and when her pregnancy came to term, she gave birth to another son and daughter. They named the son Heḇel and the daughter Aklia. First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter LXXVThe family revisits the Cave of Treasures. Birth of Heḇel and Aklia. 57

Then at the end of forty days, Adawm made an offering for the son, and at the end of eighty days he made another offering for the daughter, and treated them, as he had previously treated Qayin and his sister Luluwa.
He brought them to the Cave of Treasures, where they received a berakah, and then returned to the cave where they were born. After these children were born, Ḥawwah stopped having children.
And the children began to grow stronger and taller; but Qayin was hard−hearted, and ruled over his younger brother.
Often when his father made an offering, Qayin would remain behind and not go with them, to offer up.
But, as to Heḇel, he had a meek heart, and was obedient to his father and mother. He frequently moved them to make an offering, because he loved it. He prayed and fasted a lot.

Then came this sign to Heḇel. As he was coming into the Cave of Treasures, and saw the golden rods, the incense and the myrrh, he asked his parents, Adawm and Ḥawwah, to tell him about them and asked, "Where did you get these from?"
Then Adawm told him all that had befallen them. And Heḇel felt deeply about what his father told him.

Furthermore his father, Adawm, told him of the works of Aluahim, and of the garden. After hearing that, Heḇel remained behind after his father left and stayed the whole of that night in the Cave of Treasures.

And that night, while he was praying, Satan appeared to him under the figure of a man, who said to him, "You have frequently moved your father into making offerings, fasting and praying, therefore I will kill you, and make you perish from this world."

But as for Heḇel, he prayed to Aluahim, and drove away Satan from him; and did not believe the words of the devil. Then when it was day, an mal'ak of Aluahim appeared to him, who said to him, "Do not cut short either fasting, prayer, or offering up an offering to your Aluahim. For, look, Yahuah had accepted your prayer. Be not afraid of the figure which appeared to you in the night, and who cursed you to death." And the mal'ak departed from him.

Then when it was day, Heḇel came to Adawm and Ḥawwah, and told them of the vision he had seen. When they heard it, they grieved much over it, but said nothing to him about it; they only comforted him.

But as to the hard−hearted Qayin, Satan came to him by night, showed himself and said to him, "Since Adawm and Ḥawwah love your brother Heḇel so much more than they love you, they wish to join him in marriage to your beautiful sister because they love him. However, they wish to join you in marriage to his ugly sister, because they hate you.
Now before they do that, I am telling you that you should kill your brother. That way your sister will be left for you, and his sister will be cast away."

And Satan departed from him. But the devil remained behind in Qayin's heart, and frequently aspired to kill his brother. First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter LXXVIQayin becomes jealous of Heḇel because of his sisters. 58

But when Adawm saw that the older brother hated the younger, he endeavored to soften their hearts, and said to Qayin, "O my son, take of the fruits of your sowing and make an offering to Aluahim, so that He might forgive you for your wickedness and sin."
He said also to Heḇel, "Take some of your sowing and make an offering and bring it to Aluahim, so that He might forgive you for your wickedness and sin."
Then Heḇel obeyed his father's voice, took some of his sowing, and made a good offering, and said to his father, Adawm, "Come with me and show me how to offer it up."
And they went, Adawm and Ḥawwah with him, and they showed him how to offer up his gift on the mizbe'ach. Then after that, they stood up and prayed that Aluahim would accept Heḇel's offering.
Then Aluahim looked at Heḇel and accepted his offering. And Aluahim was more pleased with Heḇel than with his offering, because of his good heart and pure body. There was no trace of guile in him.

Then they came down from the mizbe'ach, and went to the cave in which they lived. But Heḇel, by reason of his joy at having made his offering, repeated it three times a week, after the example of his father Adawm.

But as to Qayin, he did not want to make an offering, but after his father became very angry, he offered up a gift once. He took the smallest of his sheep for an offering and when he offered it up, his eyes were on the lamb.
Therefore Aluahim did not accept his offering, because his heart was full of murderous thoughts.
And they all thus lived together in the cave in which Ḥawwah had brought forth, until Qayin was fifteen years old, and Heḇel twelve years old.
Then Adawm said to Ḥawwah, "Behold the children are grown up; we must think of finding wives for them."
Then Ḥawwah answered, "How can we do it?" 3 Then Adawm said to her, "We will join Heḇel's sister in marriage to Qayin, and Qayin's sister to Heḇel.
The said Ḥawwah to Adawm, "I do not like Qayin because he is hard−hearted; but let them stay with us until we offer up to Yahuah in their behalf."
And Adawm said no more.

Meanwhile Satan came to Qayin in the figure of a man of the field, and said to him, "Behold Adawm and Ḥawwah have taken counsel together about the marriage of you two; and they have agreed to marry Heḇel's sister to you, and your sister to him.

But if it was not that I love you, I would not have told you this thing. Yet if you will take my advice, and obey me, I will bring to you on your wedding day beautiful robes, gold and silver in plenty, and my relations will attend you." First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter LXXVIIQayin, 15 years old, and Heḇel 12 years old, grow apart. 59
Then Qayin said with joy, "Where are your relations?"
And Satan answered, "My relations are in a garden in the north, where I once meant to bring your father Adawm; but he would not accept my offer.
But you, if you will receive my words and if you will come to me after your wedding, you shall rest from the misery in which you are; and you shall rest and be better off than your father Adawm."
At these words of Satan Qayin opened his ears, and leaned towards his speech.
And he did not remain in the field, but he went to Ḥawwah, his mother, and beat her, and cursed her, and said to her, "Why are you planning to take my sister to wed her to my brother? Am I dead?"
His mother, however, quieted him, and sent him to the field where he had been.
Then when Adawm came, she told him of what Qayin had done.
But Adawm grieved and held his shalom, and said not a word.

Then on the next morning Adawm said to Qayin his son, "Take of your sheep, young and good, and offer them up to your Aluahim; and I will speak to your brother, to make to his Aluahim an offering of corn."
They both obeyed their father Adawm, and they took their offerings, and offered them up on the mountain by the mizbe'ach.

But Qayin behaved haughtily towards his brother, and shoved him from the mizbe'ach, and would not let him offer up his gift on the mizbe'ach; but he offered his own on it, with a proud heart, full of guile, and fraud.
But as for Heḇel, he set up stones that were near at hand, and on that, he offered up his gift with a heart humble and free from guile.

Qayin was then standing by the mizbe'ach on which he had offered up his gift; and he cried to Aluahim to accept his offering; but Aluahim did not accept it from him; neither did a divine fire come down to consume his offering.
But he remained standing over against the mizbe'ach, out of humor and meanness, looking towards his brother Heḇel, to see if Aluahim would accept his offering or not.

And Heḇel prayed to Aluahim to accept his offering. Then a divine fire came down and consumed his offering. And Aluahim smelled the sweet savor of his offering; because Heḇel loved Him and rejoice in Him.

And because Aluahim was well pleased with him, He sent him an mal'ak of light in the figure of a man who had partaken of his offering, because He had smelled the sweet savor of his offering, and they comforted Heḇel and strengthened his heart.
But Qayin was looking on all that took place at his brother's offering, and was angry because of it.
Then he opened his mouth and blasphemed Aluahim, because He had not accepted his offering.

But Aluahim said to Qayin, "Why do you look sad? Be righteous, that I may accept your offering. Not against Me have you murmured, but against yourself. 27 And Aluahim said this to Qayin in rebuke, and because He abhorred him and his offering. First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter LXXVIIQayin, 15 years old, and Heḇel 12 years old, grow apart. 60

And Qayin came down from the mizbe'ach, his color changed and with a sad face, and came to his father and mother and told them all that had befallen him. And Adawm grieved much because Aluahim had not accepted Qayin's offering.
But Heḇel came down rejoicing, and with a gladsome heart, and told his father and mother how Aluahim had accepted his offering. And they rejoiced at it and kissed his face.
And Heḇel said to his father, "Because Qayin shoved me from the mizbe'ach, and would not allow me to offer my gift on it, I made an mizbe'ach for myself and offered my gift on it."
But when Adawm heard this he was very sorry, because it was the mizbe'ach he had built at first, and on which he had offered his own gifts.

As to Qayin, he was so resentful and so angry that he went into the field, where Satan came to him and said to him, "Since your brother Heḇel has taken refuge with your father Adawm, because you shoved him from the mizbe'ach, they have kissed his face, and they rejoice over him, far more than over you."
When Qayin heard these words of Satan, he was filled with rage; and he let no one know. But he was laying wait to kill his brother, until he brought him into the cave, and then said to him: −−

"O brother, the country is so beautiful, and there are such beautiful and pleasurable trees in it, and charming to look at! But brother, you have never been one day in the field to take your pleasure in that place.

Today, O, my brother, I very much wish you would come with me into the field, to enjoy yourself and to bless our fields and our flocks, for you are righteous, and I love you much, O my brother! But you have alienated yourself from me."
Then Heḇel consented to go with his brother Qayin into the field.
But before going out, Qayin said to Heḇel, "Wait for me, until I fetch a staff, because of wild beasts."

Then Heḇel stood waiting in his innocence. But Qayin, the forward, fetched a staff and went out. 39 And they began, Qayin and his brother Heḇel, to walk in the way; Qayin talking to him, and comforting him, to make him forget everything.

And so they went on, until they came to a lonely place, where there were no sheep; then Heḇel said to Qayin, "Behold, my brother, we are tired from walking; for we see none of the trees, nor of the fruits, nor of the flourishing green plants, nor of the sheep, nor any one of the things of which you told me. Where are those sheep of thine you told me to bless?"
Then Qayin said to him, "Come on, and you shall see many beautiful things very soon, but go before me, until I catch up to you."
Then went Heḇel forward, but Qayin remained behind him.

And Heḇel was walking in his innocence, without guile; not believing his brother would kill him. First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter LXXIXA wicked plan is carried to a tragic conclusion. Qayin is frightened. "Am I my brother's keeper?" The seven punishments. Shalom is shattered.61
Then Qayin, when he came up to him, comforted him with his talk, walking a little behind him; then he ran up to him and beat him with the staff, blow after blow, until he was stunned.

But when Heḇel fell down on the ground, seeing that his brother meant to kill him, he said to Qayin, "O, my brother, have pity on me. By the breasts we have sucked, don't hit me! By the womb that bore us and that brought us into the world, don't beat me to death with that staff! If you will kill me, take one of these large stones and kill me outright."
Then Qayin, the hard−hearted, and cruel murderer, took a large stone, and beat his brother's head with it, until his brains oozed out, and he wallowed in his blood, before him.
And Qayin repented not of what he had done.
But the earth, when the blood of righteous Heḇel fell on it, trembled, as it drank his blood, and would have destroyed Qayin because of it.
And the blood of Heḇel cried mysteriously to Aluahim, to avenge him of his murderer.
Then Qayin began at once to dig the ground wherein to lay his brother; for he was trembling from the fear that came over him, when he saw the earth tremble on his account.
He then cast his brother into the pit he made, and covered him with dust. But the ground would not receive him; but it threw him up at once.
Again Qayin dug the ground and hid his brother in it; but again the ground threw him up on itself; until three times the ground thus threw up on itself the body of Heḇel.

The muddy ground threw him up the first time, because he was not the first creation; and it threw him up the second time and would not receive him, because he was righteous and good, and was killed without a cause; and the ground threw him up the third time and would not receive him, that there might remain before his brother a witness against him.
And so the earth mocked Qayin, until the Word of Aluahim, came to him concerning his brother.

Then was Aluahim angry, and much displeased at Heḇel's death; and He thundered from shamayim, and lightnings went before Him, and the Word of Yahuah Aluahim came from shamayim to Qayin, and said to him, "Where is Heḇel your brother?"
Then Qayin answered with a proud heart and a gruff voice, "How, O Aluahim? Am I my brother's keeper?"

Then Aluahim said to Qayin, "Cursed be the earth that has drunk the blood of Heḇel your brother; and as for you, you will always be trembling and shaking; and this will be a mark on you so that whoever finds you, will kill you."
But Qayin cried because Aluahim had said those words to him; and Qayin said to Him, "O Aluahim, whosoever finds me shall kill me, and I shall be blotted out from the face of the earth."

Then Aluahim said to Qayin, "Whoever finds you will not kill you;" because before this, Aluahim had been saying to Qayin, "I shall put seven punishments on anyone that kills Qayin." For as to the word of Aluahim to Qayin, "Where is your brother?" Aluahim said it in mercy for him, to try and make him repent. First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter LXXIXA wicked plan is carried to a tragic conclusion. Qayin is frightened. "Am I my brother's keeper?" The seven punishments. Shalom is shattered.62
For if Qayin had repented at that time, and had said, "O Aluahim, forgive me my sin, and the murder of my brother," Aluahim would then have forgiven him his sin.

And as to Aluahim saying to Qayin, "Cursed be the ground that has drunk the blood of your brother" That also, was Aluahim's mercy on Qayin. For Aluahim did not curse him, but He cursed the ground; although it was not the ground that had killed Heḇel, and committed a wicked sin.
For it was fitting that the curse should fall on the murderer; yet in mercy did Aluahim so manage His thoughts as that no one should know it, and turn away from Qayin.
And He said to him, "Where is your brother?" To which he answered and said, "I know not." Then the Creator said to him, "Be trembling and quaking."

Then Qayin trembled and became terrified; and through this sign did Aluahim make him an example before all the creation, as the murderer of his brother. Also did Aluahim bring trembling and terror over him, that he might see the shalom in which he was at first, and see also the trembling and terror he endured at the last; so that he might humble himself before Aluahim, and repent of his sin, and seek the shalom that he enjoyed at first.

And in the word of Aluahim that said, "I will put seven punishments on anyone who kills Qayin," Aluahim was not seeking to kill Qayin with the sword, but He sought to make him die of fasting, and praying and crying by hard rule, until the time that he was delivered from his sin.
And the seven punishments are the seven generations during which Aluahim awaited Qayin for the murder of his brother.

But as to Qayin, ever since he had killed his brother, he could find no rest in any place; but went back to Adawm and Ḥawwah, trembling, terrified, and defiled with blood. . . . End of First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah First Book of Adawm and Ḥawwah Chapter LXXIXA wicked plan is carried to a tragic conclusion. Qayin is frightened. "Am I my brother's keeper?" The seven punishments. Shalom is shattered.63
